t_ch: 听闻这番言论，围观人群无不发出嗟叹的声音。但耳尖的你，似乎在嘈杂中捕捉到了某句异样的话语。你很确定自己并没有听错。
t_en: Hearing these words, the onlookers exhale a collective groan. But your sharp ears seem to pick up s strange phrase within the din. You are positive you have not misheard.

t_ch: 或许，我可以尝试演奏。
t_en: Maybe I can try playing it.

t_ch: 真的假的？
t_en: Are you for real?

t_ch: 确实是真事，但…嗯…应该没关系，我来试试吧。
t_en: Yes, I really can... Hm... It should be fine. I'll try playing it.

t_ch: 真的假的？那你快去试试啊。
t_en: For real? Go try it out.

t_ch: 但是…嗯…应该没关系，我来吧。
t_en: But... Hmm... It should be fine. Let me try.

t_ch: 简单调试过后，水漫琴缓慢发出了悦耳的声响。
t_en: After some simple tuning, the water lyre slowly emits a lovely sound.

t_ch: 还好，看来母亲教的技巧还没有生疏。
t_en: Phew, looks like I still haven't lost the skills that Mother taught me.

t_ch: 各位客人，不介意的话，就由我来为大家演奏这台水漫琴如何？或许达不到故事里的水准，但作为沐浴后的消遣节目，应该是足够了。
t_en: Dear guests, if you don't mind, allow me to play a piece with this water lyre for everyone. I may not be able to perform at a level told in stories, but it should suffice as post-bath entertainment.

t_ch: 你好，善良的听众。希望我演奏的音乐，能为你疲惫的心灵带来些许宁静。
t_en: Hello, kind listener. I hope my music will ease your fatigued spirits.

t_ch: 我现在的名字是弥斯，如你所见，既非出身阔绰的贵族，也不是腰缠万贯的巨贾，只是个借琴声作消遣的普通演奏者罢了。
t_en: My current name is Misia. As you can see, I am neither a silver-spooned noble nor a purse-heavy merchant, just an ordinary musician who finds joy in melodic pastimes.

t_ch: 嗯…迟迟不肯离去，是还有什么好奇的吗？
t_en: Mm... You haven't left. Are you still curious about something?

t_ch: 这台乐器是…
t_en: This instrument is...

t_ch: 水漫琴，以水流作为基础动力的乐器。身形庞大，也必须摆放在靠近水源的地点。
t_en: A water lyre, an instrument powered by flowing water. It's huge in size and must be placed in a location near a water source.

t_ch: …我原本还想为您再做些更深入的介绍，但忽然想起，即便在奥赫玛，水漫琴也仅剩这一台。多做讲解，也不过是无用的知识罢了。
t_en: ...I thought to give you a more elaborate introduction, but I suddenly remembered that there is only one such water lyre left, even in Okhema. It's pointless to tell you more about the instrument.

t_ch: 你的故乡就是…
t_en: Your hometown is...

t_ch: 呵…传闻总是跑得比乐声还快。刚才您也在场，应该听到那些对话了吧。
t_en: Hee... News does travel faster than music. You were there as well just now, and I suppose you must have heard some things.

t_ch: 「卡弥特斯」…那并非是城邦的名字，而是我们居住的海湾的称呼。那是个很美的地方，轮廓明晰，色彩丰富，美得就像是幅描绘墨涅塔的画。
t_en: Carmitis... That is not a city's name, but what we call the bay where we live. It is a beautiful place with clear contours and vibrant colors. Its splendor is akin to a painting of Mnestia.

t_ch: 海浪拍打岩石，那便是最初的音乐。我们收集这些声响，将它们谱成旋律。
t_en: The waves crash against rocks, that is the very first music made. We collect these sounds and weave them into melodies.

t_ch: 奥赫玛的人们称呼它为水漫琴，但对我们来说，它就是普通的琴。我原以为这些精密的机械早已被黑潮吞没了，没想到在此处还能见到。
t_en: The Okhemans call it a water lyre. But to us, it is an ordinary lyre. I thought these intricate devices were long devoured by the black tide. I never thought I'd see one here.

t_ch: 我只是想欣赏音乐。
t_en: I just want to enjoy some good music.

t_ch: 那就请您找个安稳的地方，好生歇着吧。这里离水流很近，当心沾湿了您的衣服。
t_en: Then please find a safe spot to rest. It's too close to the water here. Your clothes might get wet.

t_ch: 向您问好，善良的听众。您聆听这乐声也有段时间了，有什么特别的感受吗？
t_en: Greetings, kind listener. You have been listening to this music for quite a while. Do you feel anything special?

t_ch: 我听到了你的「思乡」。
t_en: I can hear your longing for home.

t_ch: …故乡么。或许有吧，但在我看来，那并非是个需要思念的地方，更像是在寻找。
t_en: ...My home, hmm? Perhaps, but the way I see it, it isn't a place that needs to be thought about, rather to seek out.

t_ch: 卡弥特斯是受墨涅塔赐福之地，即便那儿已经没有人居住了，但艺术、浪漫与美也不会消失。只要是这琴声响起的地方，就可以供我栖居。
t_en: Carmitis is a place blessed by Mnestia. Even if no one lives there now, its art, romance, and beauty won't fade. As long as there is someplace where this melody is played, it is home enough for me.

t_ch: 我听到了你的「忧愁」。
t_en: I can hear your sorrow.

t_ch: …忧伤么。初到奥赫玛的时候，我确实有些慌张。全知全能的刻法勒背负天空，搭建起的小小庇护所，其中是否有我的容身之处？我不知道。
t_en: ...Sorrow, was it? I do admit that when I first arrived in Okhema, I was a little anxious. A tiny shelter built beneath the sky that is borne by the omniscient, omnipotent Kephale. Would there be a place for me here? I did not know.

t_ch: 逃难路上，我见过了太多别离。艺术有无限的空间，可演奏者只能在这狭窄的天地间求活。或许是因为这样的心境，我的琴声也不可避免地染上了哀愁吧。
t_en: As I was fleeing, I witnessed too many farewells. The horizons of art are boundless, but its musicians can only eke a living in this narrow space between heaven and earth. Perhaps it is because of this mindset that my playing is inevitably tinged with sorrow.

t_ch: 我听到了你的「愉悦」。
t_en: I can hear your joy.

t_ch: …愉快么。这可难说，我是在为享受沐浴时光的客人演奏，若是音色太过沉郁，未免有些不识趣了吧。说到底，这乐声只能做消遣用，可算不得什么艺术。
t_en: ...Joy, was it? That's hard to say. I play for the guests enjoying their baths. If my music was too melancholic, it might sour some of their moods. After all, this music is merely for entertainment, unworthy to be called true art.

t_ch: 不过，能让您感到愉悦，也是我的荣幸。
t_en: But, it is my honor for you to have felt joy.

t_ch: 我听到了你的「希望」。
t_en: I can hear your hope.

t_ch: …希冀么。那是自然，若是不抱有看到明日晨光的愿望，又该如何度过这个漫长的夜晚呢？即使奥赫玛不受黑夜侵袭，也不该忘记心怀信念。
t_en: ...Aspirations, huh. That is only natural. If we do not cling to the hopes of tomorrow's dawn, how will we even endure this long night? Even if Okhema cannot be invaded by darkness, we should not forget to have faith in our hearts.

t_ch: 聊得有些多了。要是情感必须通过话语才能传递，那便是演奏者的失职，所以还是请您找个安稳的地方，继续欣赏这水漫琴的乐声吧。
t_en: We've spoken a little much. If emotions could only be conveyed through words, the musician would have failed at their duty. So I ask that you find a safe spot and continue to enjoy this water lyre's melodies.

t_ch: 奇美拉是奥赫玛的宝物，大家都要爱护它们呀！
t_en: Chimeras are treasures of Okhema. Everyone should cherish them!

t_ch: 别看它们的体型小，但非常能干。性格温柔，做事情也认真，从来不会抱怨分配的工作，最近帮城里的人们解决了好多麻烦呢。
t_en: Don't be fooled by their small size. They're very capable. Gentle, but serious about their tasks, and never complain about their assigned work. They've helped solve many problems for the people in the city recently.

t_ch: 不仅这些，奇美拉还拥有非常强的团队意识，懂得相互配合，也会关心它们的管理员。
t_en: Not just that, chimeras are also highly receptive to teamwork, possessing great synergy, and will care for their handlers.

t_ch: 跟你说，前些日子，我的心情不太好，那些奇美拉就不停地围着我转，还主动用头上的角在我腿上蹭来蹭去，逗得我直笑…哈哈，能做这的园丁可太幸福了！
t_en: Let me tell you, some days ago, I was feeling a bit down. Those chimeras kept hovering around me, and even started rubbing their horns across my leg to cheer me up... Haha, I'm so fortunate to be a Gardener here!

t_ch: 听起来就挺让人羡慕的。
t_en: Sounds like an enviable position.

t_ch: 是吧！可惜成为园丁需要很长时间的培训，不然，我得要让所有奥赫玛的人们都来亲身领略奇美拉的可爱之处！
t_en: I know, right! A pity it takes a lot of training to become a Gardener, otherwise I'd make sure everyone in Okhema gets a chance to experience how totally adorable chimeras are!

t_ch: 我不喜欢宠物…
t_en: I don't like pets...

t_ch: 奇美拉才不是宠物，它们是人类的同伴！其余动物，包括那些已经灭绝的古生物，能有奇美拉这么聪明懂事嘛。我是没见过。
t_en: Chimeras aren't pets. They're companions for humans! Could any animal, like those extinct prehistoric creatures, compare to the intelligent and considerate chimeras? I think not.

t_ch: 感兴趣的话，可以常来生命花园逛逛。这里是奇美拉的培育场所和栖息地，保不准多接触个几次，你就彻底爱上它们了呢！
t_en: If you're interested, you can visit the Garden of Life more often. This is the chimeras' breeding center and habitat. You might just fall in love with them with a few more visits!

t_ch: 虽然奇美拉很乐意帮人们干活，但看到它们劳累的样子，我还是有点心疼……
t_en: The chimeras may be all too happy to help with work, but seeing them so exhausted, I can't help but feel a little sorry for them...

t_ch: 我想了解奇美拉的事。
t_en: I want to learn more about chimeras.

t_ch: 请随便问吧，整个奥赫玛恐怕都找不到比我更了解它们的人了。
t_en: Ask away. I don't think there's anyone else in Okhema who knows more about them than me.

t_ch: 关于奇美拉的来历…
t_en: About the chimeras' origin...

t_ch: 你应该听说过，奇美拉并非原本就生活在翁法罗斯的生物，而是花园的学者，使用「生命树种」培育出来的。
t_en: You must've heard before that the chimeras aren't actually native to Amphoreus. Rather, they were grown using the Seed of Life by the Garden's scholars.

t_ch: 「生命树种」记录着古生物的信息。起初学者们培育树种，是希望复活已灭绝的生物，但最终得到的结果，却是这样混合了诸多特征的奇特动物。
t_en: Prehistoric creature information is recorded in the Seed of Life. Initially, scholars grew these seeds in hopes of restoring extinct creatures, but the end result was this wondrous animal — a creature of myriad characteristics.

t_ch: 我们用传说中奇兽的名字为它命名，也就是「奇美拉」——由不同动物的部位组合而成的全新生物。是不是很贴切？
t_en: We named it after the legendary beast, the Chimera — a whole new lifeform composed of different animal parts. Isn't it quite fitting?

t_ch: 关于园丁对奇美拉的态度…
t_en: About the Gardeners' attitudes towards chimeras...

t_ch: 有些园丁坚持认为奇美拉的出现是个错误，但我不同意这个观点！
t_en: Some Gardeners insist that the chimeras' appearance is a mistake, but I vehemently disagree!

t_ch: 要我说，奇美拉是瑟希斯赐给翁法罗斯的礼物——上哪再去找这么可爱又听话的小动物呢。复原古生物只是一厢情愿的想法，我们该接受现实才对。
t_en: I say, the chimera is a gift that Cerces bestowed upon Amphoreus — where else can you find such a cute and obedient critter? Reviving creatures of the past is nothing but a pipe dream, so we should just accept reality.

t_ch: 现在，奇美拉和奥赫玛的人们相处得可好啦，大家都很喜欢它们。
t_en: Now, the chimeras get along so well with the people of Okhema. Everyone loves them.

t_ch: 没什么感兴趣的。
t_en: Nothing that interests me.

t_ch: 我想了解生命花园的事。
t_en: I wish to learn more about the Garden of Life.

t_ch: 唔，我成为园丁的时间还不长，对过去的事情可能不太了解。不过请问吧，我会尽可能地为你解答。
t_en: Oh, I haven't been here long, so I'm not too sure about past matters. But ask away, and I will try my best to answer.

t_ch: 关于花园的功能…
t_en: About the Garden's function...

t_ch: 严格来说，生命花园是个学术组织，负责研究生命相关的知识。这里的学者很多都在神悟树庭进修过，接受瑟希斯的智慧熏陶。
t_en: Strictly speaking, the Garden of Life is an academic organization charged with researching all aspects related to life. Most of the scholars here studied at the Grove of Epiphany, where they were influenced by Cerces' wisdom.

t_ch: 但最近，随着奥赫玛要处理的现实问题越来越多，花园也会帮助居民们解决些实际麻烦，譬如照料大地兽啦，消除农作物的病害啦…之类的。
t_en: But in recent times, as Okhema has more realistic problems to resolve, the Garden has also become a haven to alleviate her people's worries. They can care for dromases and eliminate diseases from crops here, among other things.

t_ch: 还有！我们组织了好多只奇美拉小队，专门为人们工作，现在特别受欢迎。你要是遇到了困难，也可以向花园提出申请，让奇美拉来帮你！
t_en: And! We've organized many chimera squads that work specially for humans. They're super popular right now. If you've run into a rut, you can make a request at the Garden and have chimeras help you out!

t_ch: 关于园丁…
t_en: About Gardeners...

t_ch: 虽然被称为「园丁」，但其实是学者。平时的工作就是在花园里培育和管理奇美拉，就像照看那些花花草草那样。
t_en: We may be called the Gardeners, but more accurately, we are scholars. Our usual work consists of caring and handling the chimeras in the Garden, no different from caring for flora.

t_ch: 说起来…我也有段时间没去树庭听讲座了。不知道他们有没有什么新发现……
t_en: Speaking of which... I haven't listened to a lecture at the Grove for quite a while. I wonder if they've made any new discoveries...

t_ch: 没什么感兴趣的。
t_en: Nothing that interests me.

t_ch: 有空再来和你聊！
t_en: Let's talk when we have more time!

t_ch: 嗯，没事的时候，也可以来花园里找奇美拉玩。相信你也会像我一样爱上它们的！
t_en: Sure thing, you can drop to play with the chimeras if you're free. You'll definitely fall in love with them like I have!

t_ch: 奇美拉啊奇美拉，你为何偏偏是奇美拉……
t_en: Oh, chimera, chimera, why did you have to be a chimera...

t_ch: 奇美拉有什么不好吗？
t_en: What's wrong with a chimera?

t_ch: 没有没有，奇美拉是很可爱的生物，也很有研究价值。
t_en: Oh nothing, not at all. They're very cute creatures worth studying.

t_ch: 只是……
t_en: It's just...

t_ch: 玫瑰换个名字，也依然芬芳。
t_en: A rose by any other name would smell as sweet.

t_ch: 奇怪，我是在哪里听过这句话吗…为什么如此耳熟？
t_en: Strange, where have I heard this line before... Why does it sound familiar?

t_ch: 算了，既视感不是重点。
t_en: Forget it, that feeling's not the point.

t_ch: 这是什么新型的精神内耗吗……
t_en: Is this some sort of newfangled mental friction...

t_ch: …精神内耗？
t_en: ...Mental friction?

t_ch: 好吧，你这么形容可能也没错。
t_en: Eh, fine, it's a good way to describe it.

t_ch: 这么说吧，如果你在几百年前就丢失了一只特夫犬、一匹提兰马、一只斑鸠——或者别的任何动物，时至今日，你还会继续寻找它们吗？
t_en: I'll put it this way: If you lost a Taffe dog, a Tyrian horse, a turtledove, or any other animal, would you still be looking for them in this day and age?

t_ch: …抱歉，我好像把事情越说越难懂了。你愿意稍稍留步，听我略加解释吗？
t_en: ...Sorry, I'm making it harder to understand. Would you be willing to stay a while and listen to my explanation?

t_ch: 没关系，你先说吧。
t_en: It's fine. Go ahead.

t_ch: 那太好了，就让我从黑潮的威胁开始说起吧。
t_en: Wonderful, I'll start from the threat of the black tide.

t_ch: 我现在正忙。
t_en: I'm busy right now.

t_ch: 这样啊…那就不打扰了。你要是对这个话题感兴趣，等闲下来了可以再来这附近找我。
t_en: I see... I won't bother you then. If you're interested in this topic, you can look for me here when you're free.

t_ch: 你可能听说过，为了复现那些在黑潮中灭绝的生物，我们在奥赫玛建立了这座花园，用于培育「生命树种」——某种记录着生命信息的物质。
t_en: You may have heard about the project. We're trying to revive creatures that went extinct from the black tide's destruction by building the Garden in Okhema and sowing the Seed of Life, which have information about life recorded in them.

t_ch: 理论上来说，用生命树种还原出来的，理应是那些消失于黑潮之中的古代生物…可如你所见，得到的奇美拉却是全新的物种。
t_en: In theory, what is restored from the Seed of Life would be ancient creatures that disappeared in the black tide... But as you can see, the chimera we grew is an entirely new lifeform altogether.

t_ch: 如果说奥赫玛真是最后的方舟…那么这方舟究竟在用什么形式保护我们的过去，就难免令人焦虑了。
t_en: If Okhema really becomes the final ark... Then, exactly how this ark is preserving our past is a cause for anxiety.

t_ch: …唉，现实如此，由不得人抱怨。至少奇美拉很有用、也很讨人喜欢——这就足够啦。我和你说这些，也不过是一个学者的自怨自艾罢了。
t_en: ...*sigh* It is what it is, so we can't really complain. At least the chimeras are useful and likable — that's enough. I'm only speaking to you from the capacity of scholarly self-pity.

t_ch: 消失的物种无法重现…这是怎么抱怨都不会有改变的事情。
t_en: Disappeared lifeforms can't be restored... This is something that will never change, no matter how prevalent the complaints are.

t_ch: 只要大家喜欢奇美拉，我们的研究…也算是歪打正着吧。
t_en: As long as everyone likes the chimeras, our research... will at least be progressing on a right path.

t_ch: 来聊聊翁法罗斯的古生物吧。
t_en: Let's talk about Amphoreus' prehistoric creatures.

t_ch: 从奇美拉自「生命树种」继承的性状来说，最值得一提的应该是菲莉丝兽、卡尼兽、还有地行龙了。你想听我说哪一种？
t_en: From the traits inherited by the chimera from the Seed of Life, what are most worthy of mention should be the Felys, the Canis, and the Terrazard. Which one do you wish to explore?

t_ch: 菲莉丝兽。
t_en: The Felys.

t_ch: 一般来说，菲莉丝兽被认为是传说中「猫」的原型。当然，也有证据说猫也是和菲莉丝兽一样真实存在的生物…不过这种学术争论我们可以按下不表。
t_en: Typically speaking, the Felys is believed to be based on the legendary "cat." Of course, there is also evidence that cats existed for real, just as real as the Felys... but let's set aside that academic debate for now.

t_ch: 和传说中的猫一样，菲莉丝兽拥有灵活优美的体型，直立的三角形耳朵和又大又圆的眼睛使它们成为出色的猎手，即使在光线昏暗的环境中也能轻松捕捉猎物。
t_en: Similar to the cat of legend, the Felys has a dexterous, elegant physique. Its upright triangular ears and big round eyes make them excellent hunters, able to deftly capture prey even in environments with dim lighting.

t_ch: 大家都认为，奇美拉智慧、狡黠的一面就是继承自菲莉丝兽的物种特征。
t_en: Most believe that the chimera's intelligence and cunning are inherited from the traits of the Felys.

t_ch: 卡尼兽。
t_en: The Canis.

t_ch: 卡尼兽是俗语中「狗」的学名；由于躯体强壮、对人类忠诚、能够在生活中切实出力，在黑潮来临前，它们普遍被称为「人类最好的朋友」。
t_en: The Canis is the scientific name of what we colloquially call "dogs." Its strong physique, loyalty to humans, and practical assistance in daily life earned them the title of "man's best friend" before the black tide descended.

t_ch: 不幸的是，可能是感官灵敏带来的副作用，卡尼兽对黑潮的抗性非常低，即便在其它动物毫无异常的环境中，卡尼兽也有可能因为黑潮的作用而突发恶性病症。
t_en: Unfortunately, perhaps a side effect of their sensitive senses, the Canis' resistance to the black tide is frighteningly low. Even in anomalous environments that don't affect other animals, the Canis would probably develop malignant symptoms due to black tide exposure.

t_ch: …在卡尼兽濒临灭绝的这个时代，的确是奇美拉继承了它们「人类最好的朋友」这样的职能。
t_en: ...In this age when the Canis is borderline extinct, the chimeras have indeed inherited the role of "man's best friend" from them.

t_ch: 地行龙。
t_en: The Terrazard.

t_ch: 地行龙是一种在黑潮刚出现的时期，就已经灭绝的野生动物，体型很小、靠吃虫子过活…但有着和奇美拉几乎相同的尾巴。
t_en: The Terrazard is a wild animal that was already extinct in the early days of the black tide. It was small in stature and subsisted on insects... but had a tail that largely resembles the chimera's.

t_ch: 地行龙非常胆小，习惯揣测人类的意图——继承了部分特征的奇美拉，在心性上也相当敏感，甚至像人那样拥有极其丰富的心思与性格。
t_en: The Terrazard was timid and adept at guessing the intents of humanity. The chimera has inherited a portion of these traits, for they are also emotionally perceptive, their wealth of thoughts and personalities comparable to humans, even.

t_ch: 顺便一说，有种观点认为大地兽就是得到了吉奥里亚赐福，并因此变得巨大的地行龙…但这应该只是两个物种在外形上略有相似之处罢了。
t_en: To add on, there is an opinion that dromases are gigantic Terrazards because they received Georios' blessing... but this is probably just due to the vague resemblance in these two species.

t_ch: 不用了。
t_en: No, I'm good.

t_ch: 行吧。古生物的知识…对翁法罗斯的未来可能确实没有什么帮助。
t_en: Fine... Knowledge on prehistoric creatures... probably isn't of much help to Amphoreus' future.

t_ch: 多干点活，少想点事。
t_en: Get more done and think less.

t_ch: 确实…如果我能全身心地投入培育奇美拉的工作，可能就不会在这里伤感了。
t_en: That's true... If I can devote myself fully into chimera breeding work, I wouldn't be wallowing in sorrow here.

t_ch: 奇美拉没有任何错，想复活古生物也是美好的理想，只是……
t_en: The chimeras have done no wrong, and trying to revive extinct animals is a beautiful dream, but...

t_ch: …唉。在这样的环境下，个人的喜好又能改变什么呢？
t_en: ...*sigh* In such an environment, what's the use of personal taste?

t_ch: 这就不行了？才跑了几步就累成这副德行？出去别说是我的学生，丢人！
t_en: Is that all you've got? You run a few steps, and you're in such a sorry state already? Don't go telling anyone you're my students, as you'll bring me nothing but shame!

t_ch: …老…呼…老师…我们已经、绕着云石天宫…跑了二…二十圈了……
t_en: ...S—Sir... Phew... We've—We've already done... t—twenty... laps around the... Marmoreal Palace...

t_ch: 啧，行吧，那就全体休息一刻钟，然后再来二十组俯卧撑。
t_en: Pfft, fine. Take a quint, then finish twenty sets of push-ups!

t_ch: 嘶——老、老师，人家讲学都是收钱…您、您是要命啊！
t_en: Ahh... S—Sir... They normally take their students' money for the lectures... N—Not their lives...

t_ch: 这才哪到哪，啊？就你们这小体格子，再不加紧锻炼，小心连学院大门都进不去！
t_en: What, too much to handle already? Ah, if you weaklings don't start taking training seriously, you might never make it into the academy!

t_ch: 不是…为啥就咱们这么特殊啊，也没见其它学派有过什么入门考核…那个投石索也太难扔了吧……
t_en: That's not what I mean... Why do we have to do this? I never saw any entrance exams for the other Schools... And that sling is way too heavy to swing...

t_ch: 当然是因为——只有强健的体魄，才能承载强韧的灵魂；只有强韧的灵魂，才能触及纯粹的真理！
t_en: It goes without saying, a powerful soul must be paired with a strong body, or it will never reach the pure truths!

t_ch: 但我们的根本目的不还是学知识吗…就您这个训练强度，怕不是要把我们都培养成竞技场英雄……
t_en: But we came here to learn... With such intense training, I'm afraid we would become arena champions instead...

t_ch: 我再重申一遍！我们的肉体乃是灵魂的容器，就像作物成长须得倚赖泥土的滋养，灵魂的完满也离不开肉体的丰盈——
t_en: I will say this one more time! Our bodies are the vessels of our souls! Just as the growth of crops relies on the soil's nourishment, the nourishment of the soul cannot be separated from the abundance of the body...

t_ch: 若是一味地追求丰产而不停耕种，却疏于对土地加以养护，那么迟早有一天，我们赖以生存的根基就会被榨干。
t_en: If you simply pursue abundant harvests and spend all your time reaping without nurturing the land, one day the very foundation of our survival will dry up and wither.

t_ch: 灵魂在得以质变擢升之前，就因肉体的衰败而枯萎——这将是何等的悲剧！
t_en: For the soul to wither and decline before achieving elevation because of a debilitated body... What a tragedy that would be!

t_ch: 即便您这么说，也……
t_en: Even so...

t_ch: 很好，既然你如此有精神，待会儿再多加五组吧。
t_en: Very well! As you've got the energy to argue, finish another five sets!

t_ch: 啊？！不是——别啊老师，我知道错了！
t_en: Huh!? No — Please, Sir, I was wrong!

t_ch: 这位朋友，我见你颇有资质，有没有兴趣了解一下我们的学派？
t_en: My friend, you look competent. Are you interested in learning more about our school?

t_ch: 介绍一下你那学派？
t_en: Introduce your school?

t_ch: 乐意效劳！那么首先，你了解「神悟树庭」吗？
t_en: Gladly! First, do you know about the Grove of Epiphany?

t_ch: 那必须的！
t_en: Who doesn't?

t_ch: 很好，这样我们就可以省去多余的介绍，直奔主题了——
t_en: Very good, we'll dispense with the preamble and get straight to business...

t_ch: 介绍一下？
t_en: Tell me about it?

t_ch: 简而言之，很久以前，{RUBY_B#最初的学者}塞勒苏斯{RUBY_E#}在{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}裂分之枝，瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的神躯栖居之处，带领众建立起了花园与庭院。
t_en: In short, a long time ago, the {RUBY_B#First Scholar}Thalesus{RUBY_E#} led the masses and built a garden and grove over where the {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Bough of Rift, Cerces'{RUBY_E#} divine shell laid.

t_ch: 那些花园与庭院后来成为了「神悟树庭」——一个供世人醒神、求识、悟道的学院，一个在学术上兼容并包、百花齐放的圣地。
t_en: The garden and grove later became the Grove of Epiphany — an academy where people awoke their minds, sought knowledge, and invoked enlightenment. A sanctuary where academia embraces inclusivity and teems with diversity.

t_ch: 在翁法罗斯，人们通常将那些具有一定规模与影响的学术群体称作「学派」，而我便属于树庭七大学派中的「曳石学派」。
t_en: In Amphoreus, people usually refer to these structured and influential academic groups as Schools, and I belong to the Helkolithist school, one of seven in the Grove.

t_ch: 我们这一派精于对「体能与心理」的研究实践——不仅在体育锻炼、竞技活动中追求「人类的极致」，往往也能在思辨活动中强有力地击倒对手。
t_en: We are proficient in the practical study of physical and mental acuities. We don't just pursue the limits of humanity in physical training and competitive activities, but also exercise our prowess in felling our opponents in battles of wit.

t_ch: 击倒…
t_en: Felling...

t_ch: 没错！就像风暴击倒大树——握紧手中的石板，给予你的辩论对手强劲有力的一击！
t_en: That's right! Like a storm felling a great tree — hand clutching a slate, punching a hole through your opponent's fallacious argument!

t_ch: 当然，就算恰好并未携带石板也不用担心，我们的课程还包括「即兴武器近战」与「徒手格斗」，以便学生们能在各种情况下随机应变。
t_en: Of course, it's okay even if you're not carrying a slate at that time. Our lessons also include improvised close quarters combat with weapons and hand-to-hand fighting. Our students are well-equipped for any situations thrown their way.

t_ch: 守卫呢，我要举报。
t_en: Where are the guards? I want to make a report.

t_ch: 哈哈，你可真幽默，我的朋友！
t_en: Haha, you're funny, friend!

t_ch: 果然知识就是力量！
t_en: Knowledge is indeed power!

t_ch: 正是如此！我就知道，你果然非常适合我们学派！
t_en: Exactly! I knew you were perfect for our school!

t_ch: 我最近都会在附近边散步边讲学，你要是感兴趣，随时可以来旁听。
t_en: I've recently been strolling about and lecturing nearby. If you are interested, you can have a listen.

t_ch: 我想听听其他学派。
t_en: I want to hear about other schools.

t_ch: 这个嘛…如果还想了解一下其它六大学派，我倒是不介意给你简单介绍介绍——
t_en: About them... If you still want to know more about the other six schools, I wouldn't mind giving you a brief intro...

t_ch: 「莲食学派」主要研究植物及其制品，但那帮家伙一个个都是苦修者，无聊得很。跟他们呆久了，怕不是正常人也要变得跟植物似的呆呆傻傻。
t_en: The Lotophagist school primarily studies plants and their products, but those fellas are all boring ascetics. Even a normal person would eventually turn into a vegetable after being in there for too long.

t_ch: 「山羊学派」专注于奇兽的繁殖和保护，但说白了，还不就是搞养殖的嘛……
t_en: The Caprist school focuses on the propagation and protection of bizarre beasts. But really, isn't that just simple breeding...?

t_ch: 还有「结绳学派」，那帮家伙排外得很，仗着自己是最早创立的学派，对谁都一副高高在上的姿态——简直恨不得在门口放个牌子，上书「不懂几何者与狗不得入内」！
t_en: And the Nodist school. Those guys are haughty exclusionists clinging on to their status as the first school to be founded, and turn their nose up on everyone else. They might as well hang a sign over their entrance that writes, "No dogs or geometrically-challenged people allowed!"

t_ch: 「敬拜学派」一天到晚就知道捣鼓仪式和钻研政治，弄出一堆乱七八糟的玩意，简直听得人头痛。
t_en: And all the Venerationist school knows is to fiddle with rituals and delve into politics all day, and create messy knick-knacks. They're a right headache.

t_ch: 还有「赤陶学派」那帮人，总嚷嚷什么「灵魂外无实体」、「存在即被感知」。有事没事就喜欢来个「信仰之跃」，不知摔残了多少学者。
t_en: And those Erythrokeramists going on about stuff like "there's no physical shell beyond the soul," "existence is to be perceived." And performing all those "leaps of faith" like they've got nothing better to do. Too many scholars have cracked their bones because of that.

t_ch: 至于「智种学派」…啧，姑且不论{RUBY_B#「智种学派」贤人}那刻夏{RUBY_E#}那些不敬神的疯言疯语，单就他那张嘴啊…为了你的身心健康着想，还是能离多远离多远吧。
t_en: As for the Nousporist school... Tsk, ignoring the blasphemous rantings of that {RUBY_B#Nousporist Sage}Anaxa{RUBY_E#} for now, just that yapper of his... For your own health, please stay far, far away from them.

t_ch: 怎么样，如此一番对比下来，果然我们「曳石学派」才是最好的选择吧！
t_en: How about it? Having compared them all, our Helkolithism is still the best, aren't we!

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 再会——散步有助于清醒地思考，有机会记得尝试一下啊！
t_en: Farewell. A walkabout helps with clearing your thoughts. Try it out when you get the chance!

t_ch: 知识应当是自由的，石板上的内容就应该无偿公开，藏私像什么话？哎，我说大家怎么就是不明白呢？
t_en: Knowledge should be free, and the contents on tablets should be made public without remuneration. Keeping it in a private collection, what nonsense is that? Bah, why can't everyone understand what I'm saying?

t_ch: 你在嚷嚷啥呢？
t_en: What are you muttering about?

t_ch: 「嚷嚷」？怎么说话呢？我在宣讲我此生践行的瑟希斯之道！
t_en: Muttering? What're you talking about? I am proclaiming the path I have pursued my entire life, the way of Cerces!

t_ch: 在浴场演讲不太合适吧？
t_en: Not very appropriate to lecture at the bath, is it?

t_ch: 怎么会呢？ 浴池能让大家心平气和地坐在一块，这么好的地方，奥赫玛独此一家！
t_en: Not really? Everyone's calm and gathered in a bath. It's the most perfect place in all of Okhema!

t_ch: 简单介绍下，我叫尤利娅，是树庭的学者，现在是浴场的常客。我喜欢在这里跟普罗大众宣讲我一生信奉的瑟希斯之道。
t_en: An introduction is in order: I am Julia, a Grove scholar, and now a bath regular. I always enjoy the chance to regale everyone here about Cerces, the path to which I have devoted my life.

t_ch: 可惜，最近来听的人并不多，可能是大家都听过十几遍了。倒是你，我看你面生得很，是新来的吧，想不想听听我的惊世理论？
t_en: Alas, not many are willing to listen these days. Maybe because they've heard it dozens of times. But you, you're an unfamiliar face. You're new, aren't you? Care to listen to my mind-blowing theories?

t_ch: 好呀，我想听听有多「惊世」。
t_en: Sure, I'd like to know how "mind-blowing" they are.

t_ch: 哈哈，我刚才老远就看到你了，模样不凡，定是个聪明人，我果然没看错！
t_en: Haha, when I spotted you from afar just now, I thought you have an extraordinary energy — must be a clever person! I just knew I wasn't wrong!

t_ch: 算了吧，我对这些不感兴趣。
t_en: Hold it right there. I'm not interested in this stuff.

t_ch: 啧，我看你样貌不凡，没想到竟是和树庭那群老古板一样的庸人，真是可悲。
t_en: Tsk, you look like an extraordinary person, but you're just as intransigent as those old farts in the Grove. What a shame.

t_ch: 其实在浴场这儿，我最常讲的永远只有两个话题——一个是抱怨像我姐姐那样短视的家伙们，一个则是我的「知识开源论」。
t_en: In this bath, I only ever talk about two topics. One of them's complaining about short-sighted crooks like my elder sister, and the other is my Open-Source Knowledge Theory.

t_ch: 你别看树庭号称什么「智慧殿堂」，什么「瑟希斯庇佑的圣地」，其实都是些虚名。
t_en: The Grove may have monikers like the "Erudite Temple," "Cerces' Blessed Sanctuary" and whatnot, but they're all just lies.

t_ch: 石板上的内容要付费阅读，会议交流也要收费入场，普通居民根本没有接触这些知识的机会。
t_en: You have to pay to read the slates' contents, and it's paid admittance for meeting discussions. Ordinary citizens have no chance to even come into contact with such knowledge.

t_ch: 可你看看奥赫玛，泰坦的奇迹随处可见，凡人有什么资格收费？知识为什么要付费？法吉娜会对泉水收费吗？刻法勒会对光照收费吗？
t_en: But look at Okhema — the Titans' miracles can be seen throughout her streets. What right do mortals have to charge a fee? Why is knowledge behind a paywall? Would Phagousa charge for their springwater? Would Kephale make you pay to bask in its light?

t_ch: 所有知识都该对公众开放，你要是想从树庭学点什么，来找我，我会免费教你。让我姐姐那种拿学问谋利的家伙看看，真正的知识是怎么传播的！
t_en: All knowledge should be shared with the public. If there's anything you wish to learn from the Grove, just approach me, and I'll teach you everything free of charge. I'll show those selfish scholars like my sister how true knowledge should be disseminated!

t_ch: 知识为什么要付费？法吉娜会对泉水收费吗？又不是我姐姐那种…哦，是你啊。怎么，想来听我宣讲「知识开源论」了吗？
t_en: Why do I have to pay for knowledge? Would Phagousa charge for their springwater? It's not like it's my sister's... Oh, it's you. What's going on? Here to listen to my Open-Source Knowledge Theory?

t_ch: 对啊，能跟你聊两句吗？
t_en: Yes, can I speak with you?

t_ch: 当然，我又不是树庭的老古董，就知道故作姿态。我最喜欢和大家聊天了，灵感总是不经意地从日常闲聊中钻进我的脑子里。
t_en: Of course, I'm not some pretentious old fart from the Grove. I love talking to everyone. Inspiration always creeps in during the most surprising situations, like banter.

t_ch: 你是树庭学者，都研究什么？
t_en: What are you researching as a Grove scholar?

t_ch: 我的研究？哦，那可太宏大了！我主要研究泰坦思维与翁法罗斯世界法则的关系。你知道吗，整个奥赫玛，不，整个翁法罗斯都是泰坦思想的具现！
t_en: My research? Oh, it's so all-encompassing! I mainly study the relationship between Titanic thoughts and the natural laws of Amphoreus. Did you know that the whole of Okhema, nay, the whole of Amphoreus, is a manifestation of the Titanic thoughts?

t_ch: 比如说，我发现雅努斯的思维模式可能影响了整个世界的空间结构。你有没有注意到奥赫玛的建筑总是成对出现？这可能就是雅努斯双面性的体现！
t_en: For example, I noticed that Janus' mode of thought may have influenced the entire world's spatial structure. Have you noticed that Okhema's buildings always come in pairs? This could be an allusion to Janus' duality!

t_ch: 还有，我在研究如何通过解析欧洛尼斯的预言来预测黑潮走向。想象一下，如果成功了，我们就能知道哪里会被黑潮侵蚀，从而提前疏散民众！
t_en: Also, I am researching on how to forecast black tide movement by analyzing Oronyx's prophecies. If I succeed, we will know where the black tide will strike next, and evacuate the people in time!

t_ch: 可惜，现在研究陷入了瓶颈。都怪那些只顾眼前利益的人，整天要我做*实用*的研究，搞得我连最基础的冥想都做不好！
t_en: It's a pity that my research has hit a bit of a bottleneck. It's all the fault of those self-serving scoundrels who keep wanting me to conduct practical research. I can't even perform the most basic of meditations now!

t_ch: 学者怎么老是待在浴池里？
t_en: Why do scholars always stay in the baths?

t_ch: 哈！你以为我是在偷懒吗？大错特错！我告诉你，浴池就是我最好的研究场所！
t_en: Ha! You think I'm slacking off? Huge mistake! Lemme tell you: The bath is the best research lab I can find!

t_ch: 首先，这里的水直接来自受法吉娜祝福的泉眼。你能想象吗？每天都能浸泡在纯粹的神性中！我在这里冥想，试图理解法吉娜的思维方式。
t_en: First, the water here originates directly from springwater blessed by Phagousa. Can you imagine that? To be soaked in pure divinity on the daily! I am meditating here to try to understand Phagousa's thought process.

t_ch: 其次，你看看周围的人。形形色色的学者、工匠、甚至是黄金裔，他们都会来这里。我可以直接听到最新的学术动态，而不用被那些老顽固过滤信息。
t_en: Next, look at the people around. Scholars and craftsmen from all disciplines, even Chrysos Heirs, they all flock here. The latest academic trends are within earshot, unlike the watered-down information fed through a drip by those old fogeys.

t_ch: 最重要的是，这里是自由的！没有人管我，告诉我该研究什么，不该研究什么。我可以尽情地思考那些看似不着边际，但可能改变整个翁法罗斯的伟大理论！
t_en: Most importantly, this is the epitome of freedom! No one to stick it to me, tell me what I should be working on or otherwise. I can ruminate on the most far-fetched of concepts that may turn out to be grand theories that can change the whole Amphoreus!

t_ch: 所以，别以为我是在逃避工作。恰恰相反，我在这里做的研究，比在那个被世俗观念束缚的树庭里有意义多了！
t_en: So don't think I'm trying to avoid work here. Quite the contrary, I am conducting research that is far more meaningful than whatever is in the Grove, fettered by shackles of worldly sentiments!

t_ch: 你跟你姐姐到底怎么回事？
t_en: What's going on with you and your elder sister?

t_ch: 喔，你说维普萨尼娅？她现在管财务方面的事，我和她的理念完全不合，待在家里只会天天吵架。
t_en: Oh, you mean Vipsania? She manages the financial stuff, and our ideals couldn't be farther apart. We'll just bicker every day if we're both home.

t_ch: 她总说什么「控制经费」「缺乏实用性价值」。拜托！你想想艾格勒，它创造光明的时候，考虑过要解决奥赫玛的照明问题吗？
t_en: All she talks about is "controlling the budget" and "lacks a practical value proposition." Oh, puh-lease! Was Aquila even thinking of Okhema's illumination problem when it created light?

t_ch: 我们姐妹俩的理念就是水火不容。她想把树庭变成一个解决日常问题的作坊，而我则要让它成为彻底开源的源泉，让翁法罗斯的每个人都能感受到瑟希斯的智慧！
t_en: Our philosophies are as opposite as fire and ice. She wants to turn the Grove into some workshop that can resolve everyday problems, while I want to turn it into a wellspring of openly shared knowledge, for everyone in Amphoreus to revel in Cerces' wisdom!

t_ch: 我没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 行吧，我也累了，今天在这儿宣讲了这么久，嘴巴都干了……
t_en: All right, I'm getting tired. I'm all parched from a long day of talking...

t_ch: 我还有事，先不聊了。
t_en: I've got to go. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 好吧，下次有机会再聊。
t_en: Okay, we'll talk next time.

t_ch: 对了，差点忘记跟你说——以后你要是想从树庭学点什么，尽管来找我！记住我的话：知识应当是自由且免费的！这才是瑟希斯的精神！
t_en: Oh yes, almost forgot to tell you — if you want to learn anything from the Grove in the future, just look me up instead! Remember my words: Knowledge should be free of charge! This is what Cerces would want!

t_ch: 尤利娅，你又在这里发呆！
t_en: Julia, you're daydreaming here again!

t_ch: 我才没发呆，这叫冥想研究！
t_en: I'm not daydreaming. This is called a meditation experiment!

t_ch: 别天真了，树庭这么多学者，吃饭不用钱？研究学问不用钱？没钱怎么做学问？你好歹也体谅下我吧，家里的生活费还是我出的呢。
t_en: Don't be naive. There are so many scholars in the Grove. Their food and studies all require funds. We won't last without money. I'm the one covering our living expenses. Have a heart!

t_ch: 别就知道钱钱钱的好吗，你的短视让瑟希斯蒙羞！
t_en: Can you stop talking about money all the time? Your shortsightedness brings shame to Cerces!

t_ch: 咳咳，这位朋友...要种子吗？
t_en: Ahem, hello there, friend... Want some seeds?

t_ch: 种子是什么？
t_en: What are seeds?

t_ch: 就是树庭的一些研究成果，在瑟希斯的祝福下，我们将自己的心血储存在特殊的「种子」上，开放授权给特定的人群阅读。
t_en: Some research outcomes from the Grove. With Cerces' blessing, we have stored our efforts into special "seeds," and are granting permission to certain groups to read them.

t_ch: 你这买卖合法吗？
t_en: You got a license for this?

t_ch: 什么呀，我这是树庭授权的正当生意，每到机缘月还要向元老院交税的。
t_en: What kind of question is that? This is officially authorized by the Grove, and I pay my taxes to the Council of Elders every Month of Fortune.

t_ch: 瞧你这一脸迷惑的模样，住在花园附近的居民们倒是见怪不怪了——你一定是新来奥赫玛不久吧？
t_en: You look bewildered, though it's a common sight to those living near the Garden. You must be new to Okhema?

t_ch: 自我介绍一下，我叫维普萨尼娅，是神悟树庭的财务官。不过现在嘛...就当我是个树庭知识的推销员吧…嗯，按我妹妹的话来说我就是「到处推销」。
t_en: Allow me to introduce myself: I am Vipsania, Grove of Epiphany Financial Officer. But now... just think of me as a Grove Knowledge salesperson... Mm, my sister would say I'm "peddling wares."

t_ch: 既然你刚到奥赫玛不久，你想买点「种子」带走吗？里面都是树庭的珍藏噢。怎么样，要不要来点？
t_en: Since you haven't been in Okhema for long, how would you like to take home some "seeds"? They're all precious treasures from the Grove. How about it, care for some?

t_ch: 「知识」也能推销吗？
t_en: "Knowledge" can be sold too?

t_ch: 当然！普通人或许做不到，但以泰坦的神力而言，这很难吗？我们也不过是沾了瑟希斯的光，得到了些余荫。
t_en: Of course! It may not be possible for ordinary people, but how hard can it be with the Titan's divinity? We've only been graced by Cerces' light, retaining slivers of their omniscience.

t_ch: 还挺有互联网精神的！
t_en: It's got an internet vibe to it!

t_ch: 啊？什么网？你是说万帷网吗？
t_en: Huh? What net? You mean the World Wound Web?

t_ch: 还是算了吧，我不需要。
t_en: It's okay. I don't need any.

t_ch: 好吧好吧，最近我们有活动折扣，你既然不愿意那就算了…真可惜啊。
t_en: All right, fine. We've got a discount on right now. If you don't want some, don't worry about it... A pity.

t_ch: 对了，你知道神悟树庭吧？人们都说，我们那儿有着翁法罗斯数一数二的聪明人。不过你可别在意这些吹捧，归根结底，做学问的基础还是做实验。没钱没平台，再聪明也白搭。
t_en: Oh yes, you know of the Grove of Epiphany, yes? Everyone says that we house all the brightest minds in Amphoreus. But don't listen to all that horn-tooting. At its heart, experimentation builds the foundation of knowledge. Without money or platform, even the smartest scholar is a fool.

t_ch: 我可不是那种倒卖树庭资源的混蛋，我这都是定期捞取「沉没树种」，看看适不适合应用到我们生活中，有合适的才好放到市场来叫卖。
t_en: I'm not one of those scoundrels reselling Grove assets. These are Sunken Tree Seeds that I regularly procure to see if they help with our daily lives. Only successful product tests are sold on the market.

t_ch: 再说了，我这么做也是为了促进知识的传播嘛。瑟希斯的精神指引着我们，让我们把知识分享给更多的人——前提是信徒们不能餐风饮露活活饿死呀。
t_en: Moreover, I'm also promoting the spread of knowledge. Cerces' spirit guides us, for us to share their knowledge with even more people. Of course, their disciples must first be able to put food on their tables.

t_ch: 怎么样，想不想为奥赫玛的知识传播出份力？
t_en: How about it, do you want to contribute to spreading the knowledge of Okhema?

t_ch: 给我来点。
t_en: Gimme some.

t_ch: 好，你可真爽快，等我检查下库存，我手头里可是有不少热门货呢——我记得有个能让庄稼产量翻倍，有个能处理生活污水的……
t_en: Great, you're decisive. Let me take a look at my stock. I do have quite a few popular products on hand. I remember there's something that can double crop yields and another that can treat household wastewater...

t_ch: 奇怪…传信石版怎么没动静了，我再检查下……
t_en: Strange... Why did the teleslate go silent? Let me double check...

t_ch: 糟了，传信石版有点问题，我和树庭那边没联系上。等修好了我再喊你，现在先把你放在预定名单里，抱歉抱歉……
t_en: Drats, there's something wrong with the teleslate. I didn't manage to link up with the Grove. I'll holler when I fix it, for now I'll put you on the reservation list, so sorry about that...

t_ch: 知识只会让我头疼，抱歉……
t_en: Knowledge only hurts my brain, sorry...

t_ch: 拒绝我推销的人很多，但这个理由我还是第一次听。难怪有些人总是领悟不了瑟希斯的精神，我现在有点头绪了。
t_en: Plenty have turned down my sales pitch, but this reason is a first. No wonder some people fail to grasp the spirit of Cerces — now I'm beginning to see why.

t_ch: 真是可惜，这么好的机会居然没做成生意。哎，都怪我没做好准备……
t_en: What a pity, such a great opportunity yet I failed to close a deal. Ah, it's all on me for not preparing...

t_ch: 今天晚点有上新，走过路过不要错过！哟，是你啊！怎么样，想来点「种子」吗？
t_en: New stocks going up on shelves tonight, don't let this slip by! Hey, it's you! What do you say, want to grab some "seeds"?

t_ch: 不过我得提前跟你打声招呼，可能是之前销量太好，树庭那边的传信石版有点问题，我现在还没联系上我的师门。
t_en: But I have to let you know in advance — there might be problems with the Grove's teleslate, perhaps due to how good sales were previously. I can't get in touch with my school.

t_ch: 所以现在是仅开放预售哈，还请见谅。
t_en: So, it's only for pre-sale now. Please bear with us.

t_ch: 不谈生意，能和你聊聊吗？
t_en: No business talk. Can we just chit-chat?

t_ch: 行啊，不谈生意就不谈呗，反正我现在也有空。你说话也好听，不像我妹妹，那么大个人了，说话就知道凶我，跟{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}亲临似的……
t_en: Sure, I've got time for some non-business talks. You sound nice too, unlike my younger sister — an adult by all accounts, but would only scowl at me like {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} themself is arriving...

t_ch: 你都卖些什么「种子」？
t_en: What kinda "seeds" you're selling?

t_ch: 哎呀，这可就多了去了。最近挺火的是有个能让植物在有风来时发出笑声的「种子」，是植物学家盖亚的贡献，条件并不苛刻，实践起来也挺有趣的。
t_en: Oh, we've got so many. Something's that quite popular recently is a "seed" that can make plants laughs in the wind. It's a contribution from the botanist Gaia. Doesn't require a lot of conditions and quite a fun experience in application.

t_ch: 至于其他的…什么类型的知识种子都有，这个得看我们树庭的学者们最近在研究什么了。
t_en: As for the others... We've got all kinds of Knowledge Seeds. It all depends on what our Grove scholars have been researching recently.

t_ch: 不过我会尽量从中选取一些实用性比较强的展示出来，这样才对得起我亲爱的顾客们嘛。免得买了一堆无用的垃圾，浪费钱不说，还影响心情。
t_en: But I'll try to pick some more practical ones to demonstrate. It's the least I can do for my beloved customers, lest they end up buying something useless, wasting their money and souring their moods.

t_ch: 能给我介绍下「种子」吗？
t_en: Can you tell me about the "seeds"?

t_ch: 唔，这东西说来话长，我捡些简单易懂的内容跟你说道说道吧。
t_en: Mm, it's a long story. I'll pick out some less-complicated stuff to explain to you.

t_ch: 种子其实就是我们树庭的一些研究成果，在瑟希斯的祝福下，我们将自己的心血储存在特殊的「知识树」上，攫取其成熟的果实。
t_en: Seeds are actually some research outcome from the Grove. With Cerces' blessing, we stored our efforts in a special "Knowledge Tree," then harvest its ripened fruit.

t_ch: 通过特定的操作，可以在传信石版上开放授权给指定的人浏览。至于这背后的原理，除了瑟希斯的赐福之外，和树庭多年来传承的技术也有关系。具体原理太过复杂，我就不跟你赘述了。
t_en: By employing certain methods, we can authorize a specific individual to browse our teleslate. As for the underlying principles, apart from Cerces' blessing, it's also to do with the techniques passed down by the Grove for many years. The exact details are really complex, so I won't bother you with them.

t_ch: 至于我，我啊，就是把这些高深莫测的知识整理成大家能看懂的东西，然后再反哺树庭的开支。说白了，就是把知识变成钱，再把钱变成更多知识。
t_en: As for me, well, I organize this profound knowledge into something that everyone can understand, then support the Grove's expenses with the money. In simpler terms, it's turning knowledge into money, and that money into even more knowledge.

t_ch: 你和你妹妹关系还好吗？
t_en: Are you on good terms with your younger sister?

t_ch: 你还真是细心，我不过随口抱怨了几句你这都能听出来啊……
t_en: You sure are perceptive. I was just venting casually, but you immediately caught on...

t_ch: 哎，我们小时候关系很好，长大了就不行了。她和我虽然同样是树庭的一份子，但观念上…只能说分歧很大。
t_en: *sigh* We were very close as kids, but not after we grew up. We may both be from the Grove, but in terms of perspective... I can only say that we don't see eye to eye.

t_ch: 她觉得一切知识都要免费开放，这才符合瑟希斯的精神。但理想和生活总归是两件事，我们普通人呢，还是得考虑下生活与做学问的成本才行啊……
t_en: She thinks all knowledge should be openly shared for free, which is Cerces' philosophy. But ideals and real life are totally different concepts. Ordinary folk like us still have to consider the costs of living and pursuing knowledge...

t_ch: 可惜，她就是太理想化了，为这些事，和我见面就吵架。我能怎么办呢，我是姐姐，总得让着她一些…等她过几年成熟点了或许就懂我的苦心了吧。
t_en: It's sad. She's just too idealistic, and constantly fights with me on these matters. What can I do? I'm the elder sister, so I have to give in to her... She'll understand what I'm trying to do when she's a bit older.

t_ch: 我没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 我还有事，先不聊了。
t_en: I've got to go. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 好，我也得忙我的工作去了。
t_en: Okay, I have to get back to work too.

t_ch: 对了，你要是有什么想学的，可以随时来找我 ，我看看能不能帮你从万帷网上搞点「种子」下来。毕竟，知识就是力量。而我这儿，可是有最直接的力量来源呢！
t_en: Oh yeah, if you have anything you wish to learn, you can look for me anytime. I'll see if I can help you acquire some "seeds" from the World Wound Web. After all, knowledge is power. And I am your direct source to this power!

t_ch: 嗝…这么盛大的日子，当然要来一杯…嗝，鲜血蜜酿了。敬欧利庞王和他的勇士们！
t_en: *hic*... Such a momentous day, of course we must have a drink with... *hic* sanguine honey brew. Praise King Eurypon and his warriors!

t_ch: 你为什么在打嗝？
t_en: Why are you hiccupping?

t_ch: 别在意…嗝，我喝得太快了。庆典上的鲜血蜜酿果然甘美非凡。虽然只是石榴汁，但在庆典上喝总会激发出不一样的味道。
t_en: Don't mind me... *hic* I just drank too quickly. The sanguine honey brew at the celebration is sweet as nectar itself. It may just be pomegranate juice, but it surely evokes a different flavor when drank at a feast.

t_ch: 鲜血蜜酿？
t_en: Sanguine honey brew?

t_ch: 是啊…嗝，悬锋勇士指定饮品。虽然只是石榴汁，但在庆典上喝总会激发出不一样的味道，甘美非凡。
t_en: Yes... *hic* The designated beverage of Kremnos warriors. It may just be pomegranate juice, but drinking it at a feast brings out an extraordinarily sweet flavor within.

t_ch: 真正的悬锋战士就该痛饮鲜血！…不过像我这样的人没什么机会踏上战场，只能用蜜酿来替代了。
t_en: True Kremnos warriors should slake their thirst in blood! ...But for someone like me who'd never get a chance to step foot on the battlefield, I'll have to make do with this honey brew.

t_ch: 这东西能活络身心，补血化瘀。不管有伤没伤，每天来上一杯准没错。待会儿等你参与完试炼，我也帮你准备一杯——如果你能活着出来的话，哈哈！
t_en: This stuff invigorates both body and mind, enriches your blood and improves its circulation. Wounds or no, you can't go wrong with a cup a day. When you're done with the trial, I'll help you prep a cup too — if you make it out alive, that is. Haha!

t_ch: 喝下这杯鲜血秘酿…嗝…拥抱荣耀与力量吧！
t_en: Drink this sanguine honey brew... *hic* ...and embrace glory and strength!

t_ch: 我有些问题想问…
t_en: I still have some more questions...

t_ch: 可以，但是…嗝…我说话可能比较慢，你得耐心。
t_en: Sure, but... *hic* My speech is a little impaired, so do be patient.

t_ch: 为什么管石榴汁叫鲜血？
t_en: Why do you call pomegranate juice fresh blood?

t_ch: 你是说鲜血蜜酿？哈哈…嗝…告诉你一个吓人的秘密——以前这玩意可不是石榴做的。是真是假我不知道。
t_en: You mean this sanguine honey brew? Haha... *hic* ...Lemme tell you a horrifying secret — this stuff didn't use to be made from pomegranates, though I wouldn't know if this was true.

t_ch: 现在我们喝的蜜酿是法吉娜信徒的手笔。当年尼卡多利被法吉娜羞辱，便勒令它献上城邦居民的鲜血，不然就亲手去取……
t_en: The honey brew we're drinking now is a creation of Phagousa's disciples. Back when Nikador was humiliated by Phagousa, the former ordered the latter to offer their citizens' blood as an apology, or they'd personally drain the blood from the people...

t_ch: 法吉娜的生性并不残暴。作为替代，它向尼卡多利献上与鲜血颜色相近的石榴汁，借由信徒之手发放给了每一个悬锋城人…嗝，确实好喝。仔细想想，真正的鲜血应该又腥又咸吧……
t_en: Phagousa's nature isn't so brutal. In replacement, they gave Nikador pomegranate juice which looked a lot like blood, and had their disciples distribute them into the hands of everyone in Castrum Kremnos... *hic* This sure is tasty. When you think about it, actual blood probably tastes all fishy and salty, no...?

t_ch: 总之，尼卡多利品尝了那带有神力的佳酿，感到十分满意，便以蜜酿的制作工艺为条件，收回了对法吉娜城邦的惩戒。
t_en: Anyway, Nikador had a sip of this fine brew imbued with divinity, expressed utter satisfaction, and rescinded the punishment on Phagousa's city, with knowledge of the honey brew's production as a condition.

t_ch: 后来悬锋人对配方加以改进，才有了今天我们喝到的口味，就连那些法吉娜信徒都没法完全复刻！
t_en: The Kremnoans later improved upon this recipe, turning it into what we drink today. Even Phagousa's disciples can't get its flavor down completely!

t_ch: …唉，那会儿的尼卡多利是多么英明神武，仅靠矛尖一指就能屈人之兵。再看现在……
t_en: ...*sigh* Back then, Nikador was so valiant and mighty, able to subdue entire armies with just their spear tip. But now...

t_ch: 你喝这么多没关系吗？
t_en: You sure you're fine drinking so much?

t_ch: 没事~即便这蜜酿蕴含法吉娜的赐福，最多也只会让人沉溺，不至于醉倒……
t_en: I'm awright... Even if this honey brew was blessed by Phagousa, it'll only keep one heartily indulged without any of the drunkenness...

t_ch: 何况对悬锋战士来说，肚量也是战斗力的一部分——英雄不光要能打，还要能喝，不然怎么撑得过庆功宴！
t_en: And to a Kremnos Warrior, a belly's capacity is also part of battle ability — heroes must be good at both fighting and drinking! How else would we last through the victory banquet!

t_ch: 再告诉你一个小秘密吧：咱们的王储也爱喝石榴汁。他还总喜欢往里面加奶，弄得粉粉的，可滑稽了！你别往外传啊……
t_en: I'll tell you another little secret: Our crown prince loves pomegranate juice, too. He usually prefers adding milk to it to get it all pinky. It's hilarious! Don't tell anyone else about this, though...

t_ch: 先说这些吧。
t_en: Let's talk later.

t_ch: 我走了，你别喝太多。
t_en: I'm leaving. Don't drink too much.

t_ch: 小意思，一会儿还能再喝一杯呢。
t_en: It's fiiiine. I can still have another cup.

t_ch: 免费的肉片麦粥，每人一份，自主领取！
t_en: Free meat n' wheat porridge, one for everyone, come get yours!

t_ch: 哟，这位勇士，看你面相年轻，想必也是来参加王的试炼吧？
t_en: Ooh, you look rather young, warrior. I suppose you're here to join the King's Trial?

t_ch: 踏进试炼场之前，得先填满肚子才行。来，把这个吃了暖暖身子吧。
t_en: You have to fill up your belly before stepping into the trial ground. Come, eat this and warm yourself up.

t_ch: 老者向你递来一碗糊状物，表面浮着一大片薄切的叉烧肉。
t_en: The old man hands you a bowl of mush with a thin slice of barbecued pork set over it.

t_ch: 你吃下了那碗粥，味道不算好，油脂很重，但饱腹感强——想来非常适合重体力劳动的士兵。
t_en: You finish the porridge. It doesn't taste great. The oil's too heavy, but it's filling — perfect for a soldier engaged in heavy labor, when you think about it.

t_ch: 怎样，是不是感觉力量从脚底板涌上了额头？这可是老奥卢斯特别调制的配方，保准你吃一顿热乎一整天。
t_en: How was it, ya feel your strength surging from your soles to your head? This is Old Aulus' special recipe, guaranteed to power you through the day with just one hearty meal.

t_ch: 祭典结束后，我会举办一场规模盛大的宴会。要是你能待到那时候，记得来捧场啊。
t_en: When the festival ends, I'll be hosting a huge feast. If you can stay around till then, don't forget to come by.

t_ch: 空腹会让你的战斗力下降七成，务必吃饱了再上战场。
t_en: An empty stomach will reduce your combat effectiveness. You have to take to the field on a full belly.

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 虽然闲聊不是我的工作，但为勇士提供精神食粮是——说吧。
t_en: Banter isn't part of my job, but providing spiritual food for warriors is. What'd you need?

t_ch: 掌粮官都做些什么？
t_en: What does a food provisions officer do?

t_ch: 哈哈——顾名思义，当然是负责管理和筹备军粮了。不过这可不是个容易的活。
t_en: Haha... As the name suggests, it's managing and stocking up on military supplies, of course. But it's no easy feat.

t_ch: 曾有贤者说过这样一句话：「兵马未动，粮草先行。」何况战争又是最马虎不得的，一点纰漏都会导致一场大败。
t_en: There was once a sage who said this: "The grain moves before the army does." You can't afford any mistakes in war. The slightest misstep will cause a crushing defeat.

t_ch: 军粮最重要的就是补充体力。我试过许多方法，最后都比不上提高含荤量——加入大块的肉。问题在于肉源从哪里找。
t_en: The most important thing about supplies is that it replenishes energy. I've tried many ways, but nothing works better than increasing the quantity of meat within, especially adding large pieces of meat. The problem is where to get this meat.

t_ch: 大地兽是重要的劳力，不能随意宰杀，因此我把目光投向了云羊和角猪——士兵们都说前者的味道更好，让我多用它做，但角猪的出肉量更高。
t_en: Dromases are an essential labor force, and we can't slaughter them needlessly. So I've got my sights on cloudsheep and hornswine — the troops say the former tastes better, and want me to use it more, but hornswines yield more meat.

t_ch: 士兵毕竟不是来餐馆点菜的顾客，有时也不能太顺着他们，对吧？
t_en: I mean, soldiers aren't customers ordering food at a diner, so I can't always cater to them, can I?

t_ch: 做掌粮官时的趣事。
t_en: Interesting stories of a food provisions officer?

t_ch: 趣事？那就不得不提之前对奥赫玛的战争中，悬锋军遭遇断粮的事了。
t_en: Interesting stories? I must talk about the war on Okhema last time, when the Kremnoan army ran into a food shortage.

t_ch: 在追击敌军时，我们正中了敌人的圈套，粮道也被切断。吃完手头的全部存粮后，大伙眼巴巴地看着我，觉得我肯定有办法搞到食物。
t_en: We walked right into an enemy trap while pursuing their troops, and our supply line got cut off. After finishing all the food we had left, everyone just stared at me, certain that I had some way to get my hands on some food.

t_ch: 我虽然心里没底，但也不能打击士气，就抱着试试看的想法四处闲逛，没想到阴差阳错地溜到了奥赫玛的营帐附近——可能他们看我不像个战士吧。
t_en: I wasn't too confident, but I wasn't about to demoralize them. So I wandered around to see if I could think of anything. Who'd thought, I somehow ended up nearby the Okhema camp — I probably didn't look like a soldier to them.

t_ch: 离开之前，我顺手牵走了三头角猪，那天晚上的全肉宴是我这辈子最愉快的一顿饭。我喂饱了许多人，也救了许多人。
t_en: Before I left, I hauled three hornswine by the reins out of their camp. That night at the all-meat buffet was the most fulfilling meal of my life. I fed a whole bunch of people, and saved them too.

t_ch: 至于可怜的奥赫玛人，他们的生产水平很高，几头角猪算不上什么损失——我还留了借条呢。
t_en: As for the poor Okhemans, they've got a rich production line. A few hornswines mean nothing to them. I even left an IOU.

t_ch: 就聊这些吧。
t_en: Let's leave it there for now.

t_ch: 感觉有精神了？那就好。
t_en: You feel more lively? That's good then.

t_ch: 我会好好吃饭。
t_en: I'll have proper meals.

t_ch: 嗯，好好吃饭，身体才能撑得住。
t_en: Good. You need to, or your body won't last.

t_ch: 敲击，呐喊，欢呼…瞧瞧这地方，气氛多么热闹啊。你也是来参加祭典的勇士吗？
t_en: Knocking, shouting, cheering... Just look at this place. Such a lively vibe. You're a warrior here to join the festival too?

t_ch: 没错，我超勇的。
t_en: That's right. I'm the warriorest of warriors.

t_ch: 不是，没兴趣。
t_en: Nah, not interested.

t_ch: 哈哈，已经赢过了。
t_en: Haha, I've won before.

t_ch: 这样啊，也好…我是没胆也没能力去为了荣耀拼杀，我还想多活两年呢。
t_en: I see, that's good too... I haven't the guts nor ability to spill blood in the name of glory. I still want to live a couple more years.

t_ch: 总之，不管你打不打算追求荣耀之死，都祝你每天过得开心——我觉得这才是最重要的。
t_en: Anyway, whether you intend to pursue glory unto death, I wish you happiness every day — I think that's what's most important.

t_ch: 宁战死…咳，还是别战死了。本分地活着才是幸运啊。
t_en: To pursue death in battle... Ahem, better not die at all. Living your part, that's what fortune should be like.

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 聊天？也好，就当消磨时间了……
t_en: A chat? Sure, I've got time to kill...

t_ch: 你在战场上表现如何？
t_en: How're you doing on the battlefield?

t_ch: 上战场？我为什么要做那种事，主动加班？加班费谁来付？
t_en: The battlefield? Why would I do something like that? Volunteering for overtime? Who's paying for that?

t_ch: 但你别说，我还真上过战场。先前对奥赫玛发起的特别行动中，欧利庞王征发了所有的士兵…我也只能被迫跟着去了。
t_en: Though I have deployed before. In that special operation against Okhema, King Eurypon mobilized the entire army... I was forced to tag along.

t_ch: 至于表现…这么跟你说吧，悬锋城的士兵总共分为三种人：鲁莽者、勇敢者和犹豫者，而我是第四种——怯懦者。
t_en: As for how I did... I'll just break it down for you — there are three kinds of soldiers in Castrum Kremnos: The brash, the brave, and the balky. And I'm the fourth kind: The craven.

t_ch: 前面几种人要么被狂热冲昏头脑，要么有丰富的作战经验，要么在恐惧和自责之间纠结；而我用影子作为武器打击敌人，一战下来不造半点杀孽……
t_en: The first few kinds are either fanatically insane, or brimming with battle experience, or struggling between terror and self-reproach. Me? I use shadows as my weapon to strike at the enemy, leaving nary a drop of blood when I fight...

t_ch: 咳咳…总之，于我而言，战争只是一种加班，被许诺的报酬是荣耀而非金钱。既然如此，何必那么卖力呢？我只是个卫兵，不是英雄。
t_en: Ahem... Anyway, war is just a form of overtime to me, and its compensation in glory instead of currency. If that's the case, why work so hard? I'm just some guard, not a hero.

t_ch: 这份工作怎么样？
t_en: What's this job like?

t_ch: 怎么，你也想当卫兵？听我一句劝——再理想的工作，时间长了也会让人生厌。
t_en: Why, you want to be a guard too? Here's a word of advice: Even the most ideal of jobs will become a grind after a while.

t_ch: 你看我，每天除了站岗还是站岗。就像把一块石头推上山坡，眼睁睁看着它滚落回来，然后再推上去……
t_en: Look at me, it's either guard duty or guard duty for me. Like pushing a rock up a hill, and then watching helplessly as it rolls back toward you, and then pushing it back up again...

t_ch: 要不是家里人说当兵吃香，硬拉着我过来报名，我才不在这风吹日晒呢…虽说确实比在外拼杀好些吧。
t_en: If it weren't for my family saying that you'd be fed in the army and dragging me along to sign up, I wouldn't even be standing here getting my face blasted by wind and shine... Though this is somewhat better than the frontline.

t_ch: 千言万语汇作一句话：给人打工就要做好预期管理。卫兵这活没机会出人头地，但也没有突然暴毙的风险，算是符合我的预期——对你来说就未必了。
t_en: Long story short: You need to manage your expectations if you're going to work for someone. There's no organizational ladder to climb as a guard, but there's also no risk of sudden death, so it's perfect in line with my expectations. I don't think it's the same for you, though.

t_ch: 先聊这些吧。
t_en: Let's end our chat here.

t_ch: 好，接下来我自己找点办法消磨时间……
t_en: All right, I'll find some other way to kill time next...

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 再见，希望你也能愉快地度过每一天。
t_en: Goodbye, I hope you live every day with joy.

t_ch: 就这样吧，你以后要是改主意了，可以随时来找我。知识就应该是公开的，免得说我不给你机会。
t_en: We'll leave it at that. You can seek me out if you change your mind. Knowledge should be shared, so don't say I didn't give you a chance.

t_ch: 聊聊你的发现？
t_en: Can you tell me what you've discovered?

t_ch: 抱歉，我有点忙…
t_en: Sorry, I'm a little busy here...

t_ch: 好吧，那也没办法。但要是有兴趣的话，可以随时来找我聊天。
t_en: Alright, that's that then. But if you're ever interested, you can stop by here for a chat with me.

t_ch: 我很乐意与阁下分享我的最新发现。
t_en: I'd be delighted to share my latest discoveries with you, esteemed outlander.

t_ch: 哎，你有所不知，外界总喜欢传些不着调的谣言，什么我能看到奥赫玛的过去啦，我的眼睛因为受泰坦的赐福而不堪重负啦。
t_en: Oh, you might not be aware, but there are all sorts of wild rumors out there. People say I can see into Okhema's past, or that my eyes bear the heavy gift of a Titan's blessing.

t_ch: 一块晶体静静地漂浮在空中，不知是什么时候冒出来的。
t_en: A crystal floats by silently in midair. Goodness knows when it first appeared.

t_ch: 喂，出来！
t_en: Hey, come out!

t_ch: （用球棒敲一敲）
t_en: (Give it a tap with your bat)

t_ch: （静观其变）
t_en: (Stay calm and observe)

t_ch: 下一秒，一股奇妙的感觉在你胸中弥散，仿佛扑面而来的海潮。你想起了在翁法罗斯经历的一切，以及曾在这里见证的那些、不存在的回忆。
t_en: In the next second, a strange feeling spreads across your chest, like a rising tide smacking you in the face. You remember everything you experienced in Amphoreus and those non-existent memories that you witnessed there.

t_ch: 当记忆的潮水退去，你发觉几块碎晶静静地躺在手心，通透、明亮。难道这就是「命运的馈赠」？
t_en: When the tide of memories subsides, you discover a few shattered crystal shards lying motionlessly in your hand, clear and bright. Is this a "gift of destiny"?

t_ch: 和往常一样，「命运」再次给出了馈赠。等到胸中的澎湃散去后，几块碎晶静静地躺在你手心。
t_en: As usual, "destiny" has delivered you another gift. Once the surge in your chest has died down, a few pieces of shattered crystal lie motionlessly in your hand.

t_ch: 是谁从过往的长河中将它们打捞上岸？这个问题恐怕没法被轻易回答。
t_en: Who salvaged them from the long river of the past? Unfortunately, that's a question that isn't easy to answer.

t_ch: 晶体表面的光芒变得暗淡。它静静地漂浮在空中，任你如何敲打都无动于衷。
t_en: The light on the crystal's surface dims. It floats before you quietly, resistant to any taps or knocks.

t_ch: 据说欧洛尼斯曾创造六个月亮，并在第七日被法吉娜灌醉，翁法罗斯一周的循环由此定下…或许在新的一周开始时，它会重新焕发光芒。
t_en: Legend has it that Oronyx created six moons before being plied with liquor on the seventh day by Phagousa, thereby creating the Amphoreus weekly cycle... Perhaps after the start of a new week, it will regain its luster and shine again.

t_ch: 这是我和丹恒以前在浴宫生活的片段，没想到还能再看见。这里的记忆浓度可真高啊……
t_en: These are memories from when I lived with Dan Heng in the bath chamber. I never thought I'd see them again. The memories here are incredibly vivid...

t_ch: 奥赫玛总是这么亮，不知道丹恒为什么能睡得那么好。
t_en: Okhema is always so bright, I can't understand how Dan Heng manages to sleep so soundly.

t_ch: 希望离开翁法罗斯之后，我也能记得在这里的时光。
t_en: I hope that I can still hold onto these memories even after I leave Amphoreus.

t_ch: 我们已经在这里住了多久呢……
t_en: Has it been that long since we moved in here?

t_ch: 或许我也该像丹恒那样，搞几本书来看。
t_en: Perhaps I should pick up some books and read, just like Dan Heng did.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，你曾在故事里见过这个庞大的木制道具，瞧它那高高昂起的头颅，以及宽广到足以装进好几个人的空洞腹部——所有特征都与你的认知相符。
t_en: There is no doubt about it. The giant wooden prop before you is one you recognize from an old story. Its head raised high in the sky and room in its stomach for several people — it has all the familiar characteristics you expect.

t_ch: 呜——困死了，究竟是谁在外面吵。让我看看，这是传到哪了…你是谁啊，找我什么事？
t_en: Wooo... I'm so tired. Who on earth is making all that ruckus outside? Let me see where this has gone out to... Who are you, and what do you want from me?

t_ch: …你是谁？
t_en: ...Who are you?

t_ch: 你怎么躲在木马里？
t_en: How come you're hiding inside a wooden horse?

t_ch: …我？奥赫玛的住民竟无知到这般地步，连「雅努斯之驹」的名号都未曾听过吗？哼哼，刻法勒号称全知全能，信奉者却如此孤陋寡闻，你真是不称职呐。
t_en: ...Me? Are the citizens of Okhema so foolish that they've never even heard of Janus' Steed? Hmph, Kephale claims to be all-knowing and all-powerful, yet their followers sure don't know a thing. Incompetence!

t_ch: 自雅努斯为天地划定界限起，我们便侍奉在左右，打开通道，传达神谕。神驹身负使命，奔走在翁法罗斯的各处，难道你就从来没遇见过？
t_en: Ever since Janus carved the boundary between heaven and earth, we have been there serving, opening passages, and delivering prophesies. Have you never seen a divine foal cantering about Amphoreus on their missions?

t_ch: 我见过你的同伴。
t_en: I've met your companions already.

t_ch: 你详细讲述了与其余木马相遇的经过，以及故事的最终结局。
t_en: You describe in great detail your encounter with the other wooden horses, including how the story ultimately ended.

t_ch: …是么。随着时间流逝，神驹的灵智也会逐渐磨损，你遇见的，或许只是具空壳。我们活得很久了，久得就像是扇年久失修的铁门。
t_en: ...Hmm. As time passes, the sentience of the divine foals will gradually dwindle away, and what you saw may have been a husk of its former self. We have lived for many a year, comparable to than an old neglected iron gate that creaks on its hinges.

t_ch: 锈迹堵塞了锁眼，就再也打不开了。唉，神驹…难道就只剩我了吗？
t_en: The keyhole is rusted over, and it can no longer be unlocked. *sigh* O, divine foals... Am I really the only one left?

t_ch: …我真没遇到过。
t_en: ...Never seen them before.

t_ch: 那就去问，去找，而不是傻傻地站在这里：你是谁呀？…这样有意思吗？
t_en: Then go out. Ask around. Find them. Don't stand here and ask ignorant questions like "Who are you." Is that fun?

t_ch: 算了，多余的话暂且不提。既然我遇见了你，还是按流程来吧——钥匙与门天然成对，想得到门后的内容，必须拿相应的物件来适配。
t_en: Forget it, I won't say anything unnecessary for now. Seeing as we've now met, let's just follow due process... Keys and gates are paired up, and if you want what's behind a gate, you'll have to match it with the corresponding object.

t_ch: 就是那个…从时空的溪流中采撷而来的「记忆」，你有吗？
t_en: It's the... "Memory" plucked out of the stream of time. Do you have it?

t_ch: （拿出记忆残晶）
t_en: (Take out the Memory Crystal Shard)

t_ch: 不错，需要的就是这个。神驹得到了满足，将为你打开雅努斯的大门。
t_en: Great, that's what I need. The divine foal has been satisfied, and will open up the gate of Janus for you.

t_ch: 我才不给你！
t_en: I'm not handing this over!

t_ch: 那你就走吧，找不到钥匙的人，不得入此门！
t_en: Then you should leave. If you can't find the key, you're not allowed inside!

t_ch: 钥匙与门天然成对，想得到门后的内容，就必须拿相应的物件来适配。
t_en: Keys and gates are paired up, and if you want what's behind a gate, you'll have to match it with the corresponding object.

t_ch: 就是那个…从时空的溪流中采撷而来的「记忆」，你有吗？
t_en: It's the... "Memory" plucked out of the stream of time. Do you have it?

t_ch: （拿出记忆残晶）
t_en: (Take out the Memory Crystal Shard)

t_ch: 不错，需要的就是这个。神驹得到了满足，将为你打开雅努斯的大门。
t_en: Great, that's what I need. The divine foal has been satisfied, and will open up the gate of Janus for you.

t_ch: 还有别的商品吗？
t_en: Any other products?

t_ch: 当然有。神驹能前往世界各处，为你取来想要的东西——只要付得起价钱。
t_en: Of course. Divine foals can travel the world and bring you whatever you need — so long as you can pay the price.

t_ch: 够了，我现在就去做准备。心怀对雅努斯的敬意，然后安静等待吧。
t_en: Enough. I am going to make my preparations. Cherish Janus in your heart, and wait here quietly.

t_ch: 神驹能前往世界各处，为你取来想要的东西——但总得给我足够的时间啊。别急别急，待到门扉彻底闭合之时，自然会有结果。
t_en: Divine foals can travel the world and bring you whatever you need — but I need enough time. Don't be impatient and hold on until the gate has closed completely. You'll get your result.

t_ch: 想要和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to chat to you.

t_ch: 说说呗，虽然你的见识很少，思想也不够深刻，但总能帮我解解闷不是？
t_en: Speak. You may be short of intellect and have no profound ideas to speak of, but at least you can help me pass away the time, hmm?

t_ch: 什么是雅努斯之驹？
t_en: What is Janus' Steed?

t_ch: 自雅努斯为天地划定界限起，我们便侍奉在它左右。若雅努斯是那至高的「万径之门」，我们便是有形的「门」——为它打开通道，传达神谕。
t_en: Ever since Janus carved the boundary between heaven and earth, we have been there serving by their side. If Janus is the holiest "Gate of Infinity," then we are the physical "gates" — We open the way for them and pass on the prophecies.

t_ch: 「门」的种类无穷尽，有的连通区域，有的跨越时空，有的分隔内外，也有的…只需要一把小小的钥匙便能为你带来惊喜。
t_en: There are endless types of "gates" out there, with some connecting areas, some linking spacetime, some separating inside from outside, and some... Just need one small key to deliver a big surprise.

t_ch: 「门」即是联结。你和我在此相遇，也是种联结。懂了吗？
t_en: "Gates" are connections. You and I meeting here is also a connection and a bond. Do you understand?

t_ch: 你的同伴呢？
t_en: Your companions?

t_ch: 随着时间流逝，神驹的灵智也会逐渐磨损。或是遭受误导，迷失在无尽的旅途之中；或是彻底消失，成为徒有其名的空壳。
t_en: As time passes, the sentience of a divine foal will gradually erode. Or it may be misled and get lost in an endless journey. Or perhaps it might vanish completely, becoming a husk of its former self.

t_ch: 自分别起，我已经很久没见过其余同伴了。神驹…难道就只剩我了吗？
t_en: It's been an age since we separated, and I saw them last. O, divine foals... Am I really the only one left?

t_ch: 为什么你需要记忆残晶？
t_en: Why do you need Memory Crystal Shards?

t_ch: 哼，欧洛尼斯与雅努斯从来不可分割，记忆既在时间上接续，也可以在空间上并存，那些碎片中存留的权能超乎你的想象。
t_en: Hmph, Oronyx and Janus have always been inseparable. Memories are connected through time and coexist spatially in space. Those shards contain authority beyond your wildest imagination.

t_ch: 至于收集这些的缘由…时机到了，雅努斯自会让你知晓。
t_en: As for the reason behind collecting all these... When the time comes, Janus will let you know.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 我才不给你！
t_en: I'm not handing this over!

t_ch: 那你就走吧，找不到钥匙的人，不得入此门！
t_en: Then you should leave. If you can't find the key, you're not allowed inside!

t_ch: 哼，神秘商品——门扉后的怪兽。你似乎购买了很了不得的东西啊。
t_en: Hmph, a mystery item — the monster behind the gate. You seem to have bought something rather amazing.

t_ch: ——嗷呜！
t_en: ...Awoo!

t_ch: ——嗷？嗷呜呜呜…呜！
t_en: ...Aw? Awoowoowoo... Woo!

t_ch: 光芒散去后，一只可爱又奇特的生物出现在了你面前！
t_en: As the light dims, a cute but strange creature appears before you!

t_ch: 没有人知道门后究竟隐藏着什么。但看来，雅努斯愿意赐福于你。
t_en: Nobody knows what lies beyond that gate, but it looks as though Janus is willing to bless you.

t_ch: 带它回去吧。从现在起，这只小奇美拉就属于你了。
t_en: Take it with you. Henceforth, this wee chimera belongs to you.

t_ch: ——嗷呜！嗷呜呜！
t_en: ...Awoo! Awoowoo!

t_ch: 出乎意料的结果…你原本还想和这匹木马理论一番，但小奇美拉已经黏上了你，还是先把它安置在你的私人浴场里吧。
t_en: An unexpected outcome... You were going to argue against the wooden horse, but this little chimera is already glued onto you — so let's stash it in our private bath for the time being.

t_ch: ——嗷呜！…嗷？嗷呜呜呜…呜！
t_en: ...Awoo! ...Aw? Awoowoo woo... Woo!

t_ch: 见到你，奇美拉似乎很开心，但现在的你根本听不懂它究竟想表达些什么，只能简单陪它玩了会，直到它的精力耗尽。
t_en: Your chimera seems happy enough to see you, but you cannot understand what it's trying to say right now. All you can do is play a while with it until it is completely sapped of energy.

t_ch: 「扎格列斯之手」挡在你面前，你试着推了推它，但它就和列车上的某些门一样纹丝不动。还是去找<color=#f29e38ff>操作石台</color>吧。
t_en: The Hand of Zagreus impedes your path. You try to push it, but it is unyielding, just like certain doors on the Express. Best that you find the <color=#f29e38ff>stone operating platform</color>.

t_ch: 到底是什么原理才能让它在空中悬停的？或许应该先研究一下它的<color=#f29e38ff>操作石台</color>。
t_en: Why and how is it suspended in midair? Maybe you should investigate its <color=#f29e38ff>stone operating platform</color>.

t_ch: 坚硬稳固的木箱，但是经不住「扎格列斯之手」<color=#f29e38ff>冲拳</color>的冲击。
t_en: A sturdy wooden box, but still not sturdy enough to endure a <color=#f29e38ff>Punch</color> from the Hand of Zagreus.

t_ch: 哼，神秘商品——法吉娜的圣兽。拿去吧，好好放在你的房间里。
t_en: Hmph. A mysterious item... the sacred beast of Phagousa. You should take it and put it on display in your room.

t_ch: 这似乎是个合适的位置，正好用来摆放你从那匹木马手中买来的神秘商品——虽然你还不知道箱子里装着的究竟是什么。要拆开看看吗？
t_en: This looks like a suitable place to put the mysterious object you bought from the wooden horse... Though you still have no idea what is inside this crate. Do you want to tear it open and see?

t_ch: （拆开箱子）
t_en: (Tear open the crate)

t_ch: 以后再说吧。
t_en: Catch you next time.

t_ch: 你满心期待地拆开了箱子，出现在眼前的是……
t_en: With great anticipation, you tear open the crate and find...

t_ch: ——巨大的海豹玩偶！
t_en: ...A giant sea seal doll!

t_ch: 好吧，你记得曾有人称呼这个物种为「法吉娜的圣兽」。将神圣形象的玩偶摆放在房间里，说不定会有好事发生呢？
t_en: Alright. You recall someone referring to this species as "the divine beasts of Phagousa." Maybe placing a doll of a divine image in your room will bring good things your way?

t_ch: 巨大的海豹玩偶，看起来就很柔软的样子。面对毛茸茸的物体，没有人能忍住抱上去的冲动。所以，你打算——
t_en: A giant seal doll that looks very soft to the touch. When faced with something this fluffy, no one can resist the urge to squeeze it tight. So, you...

t_ch: 给它一个大大的拥抱！
t_en: Give it a big hug!

t_ch: 我偏不。
t_en: (Refuse to move.)

t_ch: 好吧，你的坚定意志取得了胜利。不过，这样的战斗对你来说有什么好处呢？
t_en: Alright, your iron will wins this round. But does winning a battle like this actually benefit you in any way?

t_ch: 你将海豹玩偶抱在了怀中，充分感受着绒毛的舒适与温暖。睡意混合着幸福的错觉，渐渐从你的脑海深处蔓延开来……
t_en: You embrace the seal doll in your arms and lose yourself in the comfort and warmth of its fur. Drowsiness and an illusion of happiness begin to slowly sprout from the depths of your mind...

t_ch: 你舒舒服服地在浴池里享受了片刻的安宁。
t_en: You enjoy a wonderful moment of peace and quiet in the baths.

t_ch: …不愧是「法吉娜的圣兽」！仅凭触碰便足以令人心醉神迷，你充分领略到了它的神奇。
t_en: ...This is exactly what you'd expect from a "sacred beast of Phagousa!" Just a touch is enough to enchant you. You immediately come to understand its mysterious powers.

t_ch: 但毫无疑问，你很愿意再多来几次。
t_en: There is no doubt in your mind that you want to come back here again.

t_ch: 环境扫描完成！正在建立房间数据模型...权限归属确认：{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Room scan completed! Setting up room details... Authorization confirmed: {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 啊，我是不是该先自我介绍来着？抱歉抱歉，和你一起经历了那么多事，我都忘了还要正式介绍自己了。
t_en: Ah, perhaps I should have introduced myself? Sorry about that, after everything we've been through, I forgot the formalities.

t_ch: 我是来自黑塔空间站的XS<unbreak>-9527</unbreak>，不过现在更喜欢别人像姬子小姐那样叫我「{NICKNAME}的小助理」。
t_en: My designation is XS<unbreak>-9527</unbreak> from the Herta Space Station, but these days I'm going by "{NICKNAME}'s assistant," much like Miss Himeko does.

t_ch: 你喊我小助理就好——至于原因，我只能说绝对不是因为「小呆呆」、「小呆呆<unbreak>123</unbreak>」，还有「天才」和「天才<unbreak>123</unbreak>」这几个名字都被抢注了！
t_en: You can just refer to me as "assistant" — and no, it's definitely not because names like "Dumb-Dumb," "Dumb-Dumb<unbreak>123</unbreak>," "Genius," and "Genius<unbreak>123</unbreak>" were all already taken!

t_ch: 你怎么这么话痨。
t_en: Why do you talk so much?

t_ch: 尊重！这不叫话痨，这叫人工智能！
t_en: Show some respect! I'm not being talkative — it's just me having Artificial Intelligence!

t_ch: 之前跟你出外勤，收集到了足够多的训练数据，所以功能上也有一些升级……
t_en: After our last field operation, I gathered enough data to upgrade some functions...

t_ch: 感觉你和之前不太一样了…
t_en: You've changed a bit, haven't you?

t_ch: 因为跟你一起收集到了足够多的训练数据，功能也做了升级……
t_en: Thanks to all the training data we've gathered together, I've been able to enhance my functions.

t_ch: 目前我觉得最大的变化是——我开始有了自己的想法，就像现在，我可不想这么扫兴地跑去扫地，我只想陪着你。
t_en: The biggest change I've noticed — I've started forming my own thoughts. Like now, I don't want to just sweep floors in this dreary mood, I'd rather stay here with you.

t_ch: 啊！差点忘了，我得给你汇报一下这次升级的新功能，这些是列车和空间站为你量身定制的——
t_en: Ah! I almost forgot I need to update you on the new features from this upgrade, tailored just for you by the Express and space station...

t_ch: 首先是生活助理模式，我可以为你打扫房间，整理杂物。在有足够食材的情况下，也可以为你做饭。
t_en: First up, "Life Assistant" mode. I can tidy your room and manage clutter. If we have the right ingredients, I can cook for you too.

t_ch: 然后是通讯助手模式，目前我储存了空间站的通讯录，可以帮你接收空间站的讯息，记录重要留言，在对方同意的情况下与空间站远程连线。
t_en: Then there's the "Communication Assistant" mode. I've got the space station's directory loaded up, ready to handle incoming messages, save important ones, and facilitate remote connections upon approval.

t_ch: 再就是阅读模式，包含相册与私人文件，可以随时查阅。
t_en: Don't forget "Reading" mode, which includes access to albums and private files at any time.

t_ch: 像空间站的阿德勒科员，就托我给你带句话，他说他放弃对空间站怪谈的研究了，有份手稿——我检查过，是影印的复制品——要交给你。
t_en: Adler, a researcher from the space station, asked me to pass on a message. He's halted his research on the station's ghost tales and has a manuscript for you — it's a photocopy, which I've already verified.

t_ch: 除此以外…工程师还给我加了个唱歌功能…天杀的，这是谁提的需求？
t_en: Besides that, the engineers gave me a singing function... Seriously, whose idea was that?

t_ch: 咳咳，从我目前的语音模块工艺水平来看，我建议你没事不要启动这个功能。
t_en: Cough, considering the current state of my voice production module, I'd advise against using this feature unless absolutely necessary.

t_ch: 好了，我得更新下数据，根据你的情况做点个性化调整。你有需要的话再喊我吧。
t_en: Alright, I need to refresh the data and tweak some settings tailored to your needs. Just give me a shout if you need anything.

t_ch: 数据已经更新到最新版本啦，欢迎回来，{NICKNAME}！有什么可以帮你的吗？
t_en: Data has been updated to the latest version, welcome back, {NICKNAME}! Is there anything I can help you with?

t_ch: 切换为生活助理模式。
t_en: Switching to "Life Assistant" mode.

t_ch: 在你来之前就完成过啦，先扫后拖，地面光溜溜的就跟我的小脸一样。清洁工具也交还给列车长啦。
t_en: I finished cleaning before you arrived — swept and mopped, the floor is as smooth as my interface. All cleaning tools have been returned to the train conductor.

t_ch: 需要烹饪的话——列车长说，建议不要在房间内明火烹饪，你有需要的话最好出去找列车长交涉哦？我可不想在充电的时候被怒气冲冲的帕姆拔电源……
t_en: Regarding cooking — the conductor advises against cooking in the room. If you need to cook, it might be best to negotiate with the conductor. I wouldn't want Pom-Pom to shut my power down in a fit...

t_ch: 切换为通讯助手模式。
t_en: Switching to "Communication Assistant" mode.

t_ch: 哇！你的语音信箱差点被塞爆了，你筛选下要先听谁的留言吧？我已经按照星图地区给你做好分类啦！
t_en: Wow, your voicemail is nearly full! Why not sort through and decide whose messages to listen to first? I've organized them by astral regions for easy access.

t_ch: 顺便小声说下，根据空间站管理条例，我这边没拿到他人的声源授权，所以不能直接模仿他们的声音哦，所有内容将由本助理为你朗读！
t_en: Just a heads-up, space station regulations prevent me from mimicking other people's voices without authorization, so I'll be reading all messages in my own voice.

t_ch: 收听空间站留言。
t_en: Checking messages from the Space Station.

t_ch: 埃美丽。
t_en: Emily.

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}，可惜太忙了不然等你回空间站的时候，我请你喝下午茶！你都不知道，现在科室里的新人都说我温柔体贴...这还真得感谢你的特训呢！」
t_en: "{NICKNAME}, I wish I wasn't so slammed with work, I'd invite you for tea when you dock back at the space station. Believe it or not, all the rookies here think I'm super kind! Credit goes to your expert training!"

t_ch: 「唯一的问题是——每天都要跟大家社交，光是回消息就得花不少时间，我感觉都没有私人生活了…唔，这大概算是某种幸福的烦恼吧。哎，不说了，有人给我打视频电话，我先挂了。」
t_en: "The main issue is having to socialize every day. Just responding to messages takes up so much time, I feel like I've got no personal life left... well, I guess that's a good problem to have in a way. Oh, hold on, I've got a video call coming in, gotta go."

t_ch: 以斯帖。
t_en: Esther.

t_ch: 以斯帖阿姨最近可开心了，我听我那碎嘴子的工程师说，有不少老头在追她呢。
t_en: Aunt Esther has been in high spirits lately. According to my talkative engineer, she's become quite the heartbreaker with quite a few gentlemen admirers!

t_ch: 「乖乖，我跟你说，那天聚会我成了全场的焦点！哎呀真是臊死人了！可惜没早点认识你，要是早个几年…哎，莫说这些咯，乖乖，我准备退休了。」
t_en: "Goodness, let me tell you, I was the center of attention at that party the other day! It was quite embarrassing! I only wish we'd met sooner, perhaps a few years earlier. But never mind all that now, dear, I'm looking forward to retirement."

t_ch: 「听科员们说，你跟着列车在外面冒险，闯出不少名堂来。哎，外面很危险吧，注意安全啊。实在不行就回来，老太婆这点薄面还是有几分用的……」
t_en: "I've heard from the researchers that you've been making quite a splash on your adventures with the Express. It sounds pretty wild out there, so please take care. And remember, if it ever becomes too much, you can always come back. I still have enough influence to help you out here..."

t_ch: 维修工程师。
t_en: Maintenance Engineer.

t_ch: 说实话，要不是之前医疗科的科员给他做心理检查，我都怀疑他是不是有点自闭，真没想到他还会主动给你发消息。
t_en: To be honest, if the Medical Department hadn't given him a psychological evaluation, I might have thought he was a bit of an introvert. It's surprising he took the initiative to message you.

t_ch: 「之前的事…严格来说你是这批小机器人的再生父母，全靠你盘活！无以报答，自掏腰包给你升级了一点配置，改装了一些功能，希望你能用得习惯。」
t_en: "About the past... really, you've been like a reboot parent to these tiny robots, breathing new life into them! There's no way to fully repay you, but I've dipped into my pocket to upgrade your gear and tweak some settings — hope you find the improvements to your liking!"

t_ch: 「对了，我给它加的那些小功能你发现没？别看它害羞，其实挺爱唱歌的，就是不好意思在人前表演。」我爱唱歌？没有！没有这回事！别听他瞎说。
t_en: "Notice any new functions I added? It might seem shy, but it's actually quite fond of singing, just too bashful to perform publicly." Me, love singing? No way! Ignore that, he's full of nonsense.

t_ch: 希拉。
t_en: Sheila.

t_ch: 「老师好，谢谢你教我的那些东西，虚拟世界的东西真的很精彩，只可惜，它的阴暗面也远超我的想象。」
t_en: "Hey teacher, thanks for all the insights. The virtual world is truly fascinating, but its darker side is more intense than I ever imagined."

t_ch: 「我制造了一台…不不不，一个…一只？呃，总之就是我制造了我的复制体，老头子和站长同意了，她留有我的记忆数据，其他人察觉不到她的变化。」
t_en: "I made a... no, more like a... a clone? Yeah, that's it. I got the green light from the senior and the Lead Researcher, she has my memory data, and she's indistinguishable to anyone else."

t_ch: 「世界很简单，但人类很复杂。经过慎重考虑，我决定放弃和人类相处，这可能是我最后一次联系你。老师…再见。」
t_en: "The world is simple, people, not so much. After a lot of thought, I've decided to stop dealing with humans. This might be my last message to you, teacher. Goodbye."

t_ch: 看看其他人的留言。
t_en: Let's see what messages others have left.

t_ch: 收听贝洛伯格留言。
t_en: Checking messages from Belobog.

t_ch: 华劳斯。
t_en: Wallace.

t_ch: 「哎这是什么玩意，我在哪里说话你能听到吗？喂喂喂？这小东西很有意思啊，感觉跟我好兄弟雷金一样聪明。」
t_en: "Hey, what's this gadget? Can you hear me no matter where I am? Hello? This little device is pretty fascinating, almost as clever as my buddy Regin."

t_ch: 「什么？不卖啊，这小机器人说自己是非卖品，真的假的？算了，不说这个了，跟你说个正经儿的，雷金兄弟的成果被教育部那边否定了，我觉得他们真没眼光，申报了几个奖项也没有入围。」
t_en: "What? Not for sale? This little robot claims it's not for sale, is that for real? Anyway, forget about that, let me tell you something serious. Regin's project was turned down by the Ministry of Education. They just don't recognize talent, missing the cut for every award he applied for."

t_ch: 「我觉得问题出在奖项上，所以我打算自己搞个什么物理学奖，就用我的名字冠名，重振贝洛伯格科学界的荣光。等搞好了我喊你，说不定能参加个颁奖礼什么的。」
t_en: "I think the real problem is the awards themselves, so I'm planning to start my own Physics Science Award, named after me, to restore the glory of Belobogian science. Once I get it all set up, I'll invite you, maybe you can come to the award ceremony."

t_ch: 塔米拉。
t_en: Tamila.

t_ch: 「我最近去面试了一个音乐剧项目，内容是讲一个女舞者意外进入女子监狱的故事，故事有点黑暗色彩。果然外面的世界就是不一样，这种题材在贝洛伯格可见不着。」
t_en: "I recently tried out for a musical that's quite edgy — it's about a female dancer who unexpectedly finds herself in a women's prison. It's got a dark twist to it. The outside world sure offers some unique stories, you wouldn't come across this kind of plot back in Belobog."

t_ch: 「虽然只是女主角的替卡，但这次巡演有足足<unbreak>120</unbreak>场，经纪人给我估算了下，至少有<unbreak>30</unbreak>场需要我上场。有机会的话，你可以带上星穹列车的朋友一起来看看，提前跟我说一声，我给你们准备包厢。」
t_en: "Though I'm just an understudy for the lead, there are a whopping <unbreak>120</unbreak> shows on tour, and my agent estimated I'll be needed for at least <unbreak>30</unbreak> shows. If you get a chance, you could bring some friends from the Astral Express to watch, just let me know in advance, and I'll get a box ready for you."

t_ch: 尤利安。
t_en: Julian.

t_ch: 尤利安给你累计发送<unbreak>21</unbreak>条语音，内容均来自小学数学练习册的习题，我已经给他解答过了，目前暂无新消息。
t_en: Julian has sent you a total of <unbreak>21</unbreak> voice messages, all with questions from elementary math workbooks. I've already answered them for him. No new messages for now.

t_ch: 老歌德。
t_en: Old Goethe.

t_ch: 老歌德想向你取消正当权益会社的委托工作，具体原因你还是听他自个儿说吧：「{NICKNAME}，谢谢你对我们会社的倾力相助，只可惜…公司和贝洛伯格有一些产业合作，提供了不少就业岗位，部分社员为了多赚钱，推翻了自己从前的理念。」
t_en: Old Goethe is looking to terminate his assignment with The Rightful Rights Society. He explains it best himself: "{NICKNAME}, I deeply appreciate all the support you've given our organization, unfortunately... the IPC has entered into several industrial partnerships with Belobog, creating many job opportunities. Sadly, this has caused some of our members to abandon their initial ideals for financial gains."

t_ch: 「在几个月的争吵中，一半的社员决定与我们分道扬镳，至此我觉得正当权益会社也没什么存在的必要了，就此解散还能留个体面。所以…以后也不需要你来帮忙了，真的很感谢你，有空的话可以来我这儿喝个下午茶，我们再唠嗑唠嗑。」
t_en: "After months of debate, half of our members chose to go their separate ways. It seems The Rightful Rights Society has served its purpose, so we're thinking of disbanding to end on a dignified note. I won't need your help moving forward, but I'm deeply thankful for everything. If you're ever in the area, drop by for some tea and a chat."

t_ch: 看看其他人的留言。
t_en: Let's see what messages others have left.

t_ch: 收听仙舟留言。
t_en: Checking messages from the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 杜老板。
t_en: Boss Du.

t_ch: 杜老板每到午后就会给你发消息，说让你「得闲来饮茶」，累计发了<unbreak>13</unbreak>条，除此以外也没有别的内容。
t_en: Every afternoon, Boss Du sends you a message inviting you to have tea whenever you're free. He's sent <unbreak>13</unbreak> invites so far, without adding much else.

t_ch: 这究竟是人类的客套话，还是魔阴身发作的前兆？真是搞不懂你们人类。
t_en: Is this typical human politeness, or a sign of being mara-struck? Humans are truly baffling.

t_ch: 跃渊。
t_en: Yueyuan.

t_ch: 跃渊暂时没有留言给你，不过我看着她的状态显示一直是「工作中」，打过去也是忙音，可能是去奋斗了。
t_en: Yueyuan hasn't left any messages recently. Her status is consistently "busy," and all calls just ring out. She must be swamped at work.

t_ch: 看看其他人的留言。
t_en: Let's see what messages others have left.

t_ch: 没啥想听的，算了。
t_en: Not interested in listening to anything else.

t_ch: 切换为阅读模式。
t_en: Switching to "Reading" mode.

t_ch: 阿德勒先生送了一些资料过来，他说他准备放弃对空间站怪谈的相关研究了，他将整理出来的手稿传了过来，已经为你装订好啦。
t_en: Mr. Adler has sent over some materials, announcing he's abandoning his research into the space station's ghost stories. He's compiled and bound a manuscript, which he has already sent your way.

t_ch: 相册里更新了新照片，是你上次在贝洛伯格时，和塔米拉、塞琳小姐的合影。我给你提一嘴，我的抓拍技术不太好，希望你别在意。
t_en: New photos have been added to the album, from your last visit to Belobog with Tamila and Miss Celine. Just a heads-up, my photography skills aren't great, hope you don't mind.

t_ch: 查看《小助理的杰作》
t_en: View Assistant's Masterpiece

t_ch: 查看《离别纪念》
t_en: View Farewell Memorial

t_ch: 没什么可看的。
t_en: Nothing interesting to see.

t_ch: 没事，你忙去吧。
t_en: Go ahead, I've got things to handle.

t_ch: 噢噢，我也没啥事，准备待机到低电量再去休息。
t_en: I've got nothing on either, just waiting around until my battery runs low.

t_ch: 其实我还会讲笑话...要是你什么时候心情不好，可以找我聊天。
t_en: I can crack a few jokes too... if you're ever feeling down, feel free to chat with me.

t_ch: 接取中，请选择你想接听的联系人！
t_en: Receiving, please select who you want to hear from!

t_ch: 说实话，你的朋友还挺多的，不像我，就只认识你……
t_en: To be honest, you have quite a lot of friends, unlike me — you're the only one I've got...

t_ch: 感觉你怎么比我还自闭——啊，不是骂你的意思，我是说——你怎么比我还怕生哇，明明长得这么高大，心思却这么细腻敏感……
t_en: You seem even more introverted than me — not that it's a bad thing! I mean, despite your size and strength, you're incredibly sensitive and thoughtful.

t_ch: 这是我抓拍的…希望你别太介意。
t_en: This is one of my snapshots... hope you're okay with it.

t_ch: …怎么大家看起来都不是很熟的样子，还是说你比我还怕生？
t_en: Why does everyone look like they barely know each other, or is it you who's more introverted?

t_ch: 不至于吧，我觉得你的脸还是很好看的，值得一看。
t_en: That's not true. I think you're quite handsome. Definitely worth a second look.

t_ch: 有交到新朋友吗？
t_en: Made any new friends?

t_ch: 我这人你也知道，对朋友的要求很高。身份、外貌、职业、谈吐…一样都不能差。
t_en: You know me, I have high standards for friends. Status, appearance, profession, eloquence... I don't compromise.

t_ch: 能认识雷金实属幸运，我觉得很难再找到第二个如此优秀的朋友了。
t_en: Knowing Regin is a stroke of luck, hard to find another friend as exceptional as him.

t_ch: 你被雷金蒙骗了。
t_en: You've been fooled by Regin.

t_ch: 你是说我投资他的《民间科学》的事吧？
t_en: Are you talking about my investment in Citizen Science?

t_ch: 其实我也知道大概率是回不了本的，但是朋友之间不必那么计较金钱上的得失，何况我也不缺钱。
t_en: I know it's a long shot, but when it comes to friends, money shouldn't matter. Plus, I'm not exactly short on cash.

t_ch: 我看中的是他身上的执着。雷金这么多年一直在坚持论证他的…「大宇宙时间隧道」理论，既然我有能力帮他，为什么不试试呢？
t_en: What I appreciate is his commitment. Regin has been diligently advocating his "big universe time tunnel" theory for years. If I can support him, why not?

t_ch: 试着扩大下社交圈？
t_en: Maybe try expanding your social circle.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格就这么大，整个上层区我都熟门熟路，来来回回就是那几张面孔。
t_en: Belobog isn't that large, I practically know everyone in the Overworld area by now.
